stream_summary: The convergence/influence and media rules are, with one exception, faithfully coded to their docstrings, and the project's honesty discipline (provenance tags, the §5.14 brake-bisection, the T0.6 'BC dead channel' audit) is unusually candid about their weaknesses. But three substantive problems undercut how these mechanisms are presented. (1) BoundedConfidenceInfluence — the rule carrying the headline Hegselmann-Krause 'L' citation and the entire 'who you listen to' narrative stage — is effectively a non-force in the canonical shipped build: it emits the smallest non-zero per-tick delta of any position rule (≈0.008 RMS, below even the pure-noise term), and turning it completely OFF changes 2025 party separation by only +0.014 (~3%). (2) MediaConsumption is tagged with polarizing-media citations (Levendusky 2013, DellaVigna-Kaplan 2007, Martin-Yurukoglu 2017) but its net behavioral effect in the canonical build is the OPPOSITE — strongly de-polarizing/centripetal: partisan diet targets sit far inward of party centroids (Dem |target|=0.08 vs |centroid|=0.28 at 2025), so 'partisan media' mechanically pulls consumers toward center. (3) The only literature-pinned HK replication test exercises the hard-cutoff path (temperature=0), while the shipped build runs the author's graded-logistic+affect variant (temperature=0.05), so the 'L'-validated path is not the path that ships. Plus two dead/loose rules (ArgumentExchange unused; its Mäs-Flache mapping is loose). The code largely does what the docs say; the issue is that several mechanisms are weaker or directionally different from what their literature framing implies.
title: BoundedConfidenceInfluence is effectively inert in the canonical build — yet carries the headline Hegselmann-Krause citation and a whole narrative stage
claim: The rule that anchors the model's bounded-confidence/HK literature claim and the 'S1 Bounded confidence: who you listen to' teaching stage contributes almost nothing to the shipped trajectory. At the canonical settings (effective epsilon=0.40 via bc_epsilon_scale, strength=0.03, temperature=0.05) BC emits the smallest per-tick position delta of any rule — ~0.008 RMS in 2000-2025 — below GaussianNoise (~0.087), PartyPull (~0.06), MediaConsumption (~0.033), and even ConstraintOp (~0.02). Disabling BC entirely (strength=0) changes 2025 party separation by only +0.0144 (~3%) and within-party SD_x by +0.012; quadrupling it (0.12) moves sep by only -0.044.
evidence: validation/audit/bc_force_probe.py output: BC mean||d|| 0.0192(1980)->0.0077(2010)->0.0082(2025), vs PartyPull 0.059, GaussianNoise 0.087. validation/audit/bc_off_counterfactual.py: sep canonical 0.4693 vs BC-off 0.4837 (+0.0144). Author's own audit docs/internal/audit/t06_desaturate.md:18-23 found BC gain 0.0055 at the old 0.015 strength ('the silence is the strength, not eps'); the 'BC wake' (anes_preset.py:75-79) only restored it 2.9x to gain 0.016 — still tiny in absolute terms. scripts/anes_preset.py comment: 'at 0.30/0.015 the BC channel was effectively dead'.
why_it_matters: The model is presented as a coupled multi-channel system in which bounded-confidence influence is a first-class mechanism (CLAUDE.md, methods.md §S1, literature.md Hegselmann-Krause 2002 'the bounded-confidence core'). In the build that actually ships and is served to the public, that channel is a near-no-op: the arc would look essentially identical without it. Presenting BC as a load-bearing influence mechanism overstates its role; the convergence force the public sees attributed to 'who you listen to' is doing ~3% of the work.
suggested_fix: Either (a) re-calibrate BC to a magnitude where it measurably shapes the arc and re-bless, or (b) explicitly downgrade its presentation: state in methods.md/ENGINE_OVERVIEW that in the canonical build BC is a marginal contributor (off->sep +3%) and that party sorting is driven by PartyPull/ConstraintOp/elite loop, not bounded-confidence convergence. The current framing implies far more influence than the numbers support.

verification.reasoning:
QUANTITATIVE CORE HOLDS (verified, not trusted). I re-ran both cited scripts against the live canonical build (scripts/anes_preset.py:238 -> ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS, with tier_c_bc_strength=0.03, tier_c_bc_epsilon=0.40). validation/audit/bc_off_counterfactual.py reproduces exactly: sep canonical 0.4693 vs BC-off 0.4837 (+0.0144, ~3%), wp_sd_x +0.0122, 4x (0.12) moves sep -0.0442. validation/audit/bc_force_probe.py reproduces: BC mean||d|| 0.0193(1980)->0.0077(2000)->0.0082(2025), vs PartyPull ~0.059, GaussianNoise ~0.087, MediaConsumption ~0.033, ConstraintOp ~0.020. Wiring confirmed: historical_arc.py:1670-1677 builds BC(epsilon=0.30, strength=tier_c_bc_strength, temperature=BC_TEMPERATURE=0.05); historical_arc.py:1497-1499 sets bc_epsilon_scale = tier_c_bc_epsilon/0.30 -> effective eps 0.40. The author's OWN audit corroborates: docs/internal/audit/t06_desaturate.md:18 (BC gain 0.0055 at str 0.015), :22-23 ('the silence is the strength, not eps'), :56 (the wake to eps0.4/str0.03 = gain 0.016, 2.9x, 'wp_sd essentially unchanged'). So the literal claim 'BC contributes ~nothing to the shipped ARC trajectory' is TRUE. Since the BC-off counterfactual measures the cumulative 135-tick effect (+0.0144), the 'small per-tick but cumulatively important' defense fails.

FRAMING IS REFUTED / OVERSTATED on three points that drive the claimed severity. (1) 'Smallest per-tick delta of ANY rule' is wrong: IdentityToIdeologyPull (~0.0069) is consistently smaller than BC (~0.0077-0.0082) in the same probe output. (2) The 'public sees BC attributed to who-you-listen-to doing 3% of work' is NOT substantiated: grep of web_demo/ (rc-story.jsx, cc-pages.jsx, all .jsx) finds ZERO occurrences of 'bounded confidence' / 'who you listen' / 'listen' / 'confidence'. The shipped demo does not surface BC as a named stage at all. The 'S1 Bounded confidence: who you listen to' label lives in methods.md:47, which is the PILLAR (calm_to_camps) stage table, explicitly NOT the arc. (3) The 'whole narrative stage is near-no-op' indictment is a category error: the S1 stage's actual home is the pillar, where BC runs at strength 0.08 (calm_to_camps.py:570) -- ~2.7x the arc's 0.03 -- as the SOLE active influence force against GaussianNoise sigma 0.01 (line 568) with PartyPull/Media/etc all zero. In that stage BC is unambiguously load-bearing (the stage's predicted_effect is literally 'population variance falls relative to baseline'). The finding measures BC in the arc's drowned-out conditions (noise ~0.048 RMS, party_pull 0.25, constraint, etc.) and uses that to indict a pillar stage that runs BC isolated at higher strength. CLAUDE.md/methods.md already carefully separate the pillar (composition control) from the arc (what ships) and nowhere claim BC is load-bearing IN THE ARC.

Net: the verifiable fact (BC is a near-no-op channel in the shipped arc and carries the HK citation) is real and worth noting honestly, but the 'overclaim to the public' / 'whole narrative stage' rationale that elevated it to major does not survive checking. Downgrade to minor.
verification.notes: Legitimate residual: an interested reader who reads methods.md alongside the arc could reasonably infer BC is a meaningful arc channel because it carries the HK 'bounded-confidence core' citation; a one-line note in methods.md §5.x that BC's per-tick contribution in the shipped arc is ~3% (load-bearing only in the pillar S1 isolation stage) would close the honesty gap. Scripts re-run, not edited; nothing committed. Files checked: abm/rules/influence.py, abm/pillars/historical_arc.py:1494-1499,1670-1677, abm/pillars/calm_to_camps.py:558-588, scripts/anes_preset.py, docs/methods.md:44-50, docs/literature.md:84-88, docs/internal/audit/t06_desaturate.md, web_demo/rc-story.jsx, web_demo/cc-pages.jsx.

title: MediaConsumption is net DE-polarizing (centripetal) in the canonical build, contradicting the polarizing-media literature it is tagged with
claim: MediaConsumption pulls each agent toward its media-diet target. In the canonical build those diet targets sit far INWARD of the party centroids, so the rule mechanically pulls partisans toward the center — a de-polarizing force. Yet the rule and its interventions are framed by, and cited to, the partisan-media-DRIVES-polarization literature (Levendusky 2013, DellaVigna & Kaplan 2007, Martin & Yurukoglu 2017). The model's actual media mechanism does the opposite of what those papers find.
evidence: validation/audit/media_inward_probe.py: at 2025, Dem diet_target |t|=0.084 vs centroid |c|=0.282; Rep |t|=0.162 vs |c|=0.189 — INWARD at every checkpoint, both parties, 1980-2025. Cause: diet_for_party() (abm/core/outlets.py:54-67) gives substantial exp(-d*1.6)+U(0,0.25) weight to centrist Local TV(0,0.05) even for partisans, so the weighted target collapses toward center. methods.md §5.14 (lines 1066-1090) itself documents that activating MediaConsumption 'tethers the mass inward against elite-driven separation' and that removing the two media events makes sep OVERSHOOT (+0.48) — i.e. media is a brake, and flags the inward-outlet property as E-tagged calibration, 'not a literature claim'. X3 ('Quit cable news') buckets null (methods.md:407).
why_it_matters: The mechanism's sign is inverted relative to its cited evidence base. A reader/grader sees 'Fox News 1996 / partisan media' attached to peer-reviewed polarizing-media findings, but the engine's media channel is a depolarizing tether. The honest caveat exists deep in methods §5.14, but the rule docstring, the timeline copy, and the literature mapping still present media as a polarization driver. This is a faithfulness gap between the cited mechanism (media polarizes) and the coded behavior (media de-polarizes on the position axis).
suggested_fix: Make the inversion explicit at the rule and intervention level, not just in §5.14: state that on the position axis partisan media is modeled as centripetal (diet targets inward of centroids) and that any polarizing role of media in the model is carried only by the separate affect channel (bc_affect_weight ramp + MediatedAnimus), not MediaConsumption. Reconcile the outlet roster (Fox 0.85/MSNBC -0.80) with the fact that diets still land near center, or widen partisan diet weighting so committed partisans' targets actually sit near their outlets.

verification.reasoning:
I independently reproduced every load-bearing number and then ran a clean ablation the finding didn't have.

1) INWARD diet targets reproduced exactly. Running validation/audit/media_inward_probe.py: at 2025, Dem |diet_target|=0.084 vs |centroid|=0.282; Rep |t|=0.162 vs |c|=0.189 — INWARD at all 5 checkpoints (1980/2000/2010/2020/2025), both parties. Cause confirmed in abm/core/outlets.py:54-67: diet_for_party gives weight exp(-d*1.6)+U(0,0.25) to centrist Local TV at (0,0.05) even for partisans, collapsing the weighted target toward center; the diet is built from party_centers_active and fixed at build (historical_arc.py:1158), while agents drift outward, so the target sits inward.

2) Mechanism lens validated. MediaConsumption (abm/rules/media_consumption.py:99-112) pulls the D=7 issues vector toward lift(outlet.position). Because project(lift(xy))==xy (abm/core/issues.py:145-152), the projected 2D pull equals strength*(diet_target_2D - pos_2D) exactly, so the probe's 2D comparison is the correct lens for the compass/position axis.

3) Per-tick directional measurement (mine): at every active year 1988-2025 a single media tick DECREASES the Rep-Dem centroid gap (d = -0.003 to -0.008). Net centripetal on the separation axis, not just on the mean.

4) Clean A/B ablation (mine, decisive): same seed/schedule/penetration series, MediaConsumption.apply forced to no-op vs canonical. Media ON: party-axis sep 0.643, centroid gap 0.469. Media OFF: party-axis sep 0.841, centroid gap 0.629. Turning media ON REDUCES party-axis separation by 0.198 and the centroid gap by 0.160. Media is unambiguously a de-polarizing brake on the position/sorting axis. (My first A/B that set env.attrs media_strength=0 showed null because MediaPenetrationSeries overwrites it each step — disabling the rule itself is the correct method.)

5) Author's own corroboration: docs/internal/audit/events_brake_bisection.md shows AOEB restraint +0.456 for Fox'96 alone and +0.261 for Fairness Doctrine'87 alone (both set MediaConsumption.strength), joint media events +0.446 (93% of the +0.482 overshoot). methods.md:1066-1090 explicitly states partisan media 'tethers the mass inward against elite-driven separation' and flags the inward-outlet property as E-tagged 'not a literature claim'.

6) Faithfulness gap confirmed. literature.md:328 attaches X3 'Quit cable news' / MediaConsumption to Levendusky 2013, DellaVigna & Kaplan 2007 (partisan-media-polarizes literature); polarization_causal_model.md:138 frames media as the amplifier in elite->media->mass. The coded position-axis behavior is the opposite.

FAIR SCOPING (does not refute, but bounds severity): the inversion is specific to the POSITION/sorting axis. The model's polarizing-media effect lives on the AFFECT axis via MediatedAnimus (abm/rules/mediated_animus.py), scaled by mediated_animus_weight (cable/Fox/social-media exposure); the bisection shows social_media_ramp contributes +0.165 affect. So media does polarize affect in the model. The finding's text already scopes its claim to the position axis, so it is accurate. Severity stays major: it is a real sign inversion of a named, literature-cited mechanism on the positional channel, the honest caveat is buried in methods §5.14 while the rule docstring/timeline/literature-map present media as a driver, and several cited papers (Levendusky 2013, DellaVigna & Kaplan 2007, Martin & Yurukoglu 2017) concern positional/vote effects, not solely affect.
verification.notes: Files checked: abm/core/outlets.py:54-67; abm/rules/media_consumption.py:99-125; abm/core/issues.py:145-175; abm/pillars/historical_arc.py:1000-1002,1158,1270; docs/methods.md:1058-1096; docs/internal/audit/events_brake_bisection.md; docs/literature.md:328-329; docs/polarization_causal_model.md:138; abm/rules/mediated_animus.py:1-40. Probe reproduced + extended with a per-tick gap measurement and a clean rule-disable A/B (media ON sep 0.643 vs OFF 0.841). Not refutable: the de-polarizing sign on the position axis is robust across three independent methods plus the author's own ablation artifact. Only correction to the finding is a scoping clarification: the model does carry polarizing media on the AFFECT axis (MediatedAnimus), so 'the model's media mechanism does the opposite of the papers' is true specifically for the positional MediaConsumption channel, which is how the finding already frames it.

title: The literature-pinned HK replication test validates a path the canonical build does not use
claim: The only literature-pinned Hegselmann-Krause replication (tests/test_canonical.py) exercises the hard-cutoff branch (temperature=0). The canonical shipped build runs BC with temperature=0.05 (the graded-logistic 'Phase 4 F2' filter) plus an affect modulator — an author-designed variant of HK, not the classic mechanism. So the 'L (Hegselmann-Krause)' provenance and the 'recovered exactly as the complete-graph special case' claim attach to a code path the public build never takes.
evidence: tests/test_canonical.py docstring: 'Canonical replication — Hegselmann-Krause phase behavior'; tests/test_phase4.py:259 test_graded_filter_at_zero_temperature_matches_hard_cutoff confirms the two branches differ for temperature>0. Canonical build uses temperature=BC_TEMPERATURE=0.05 (calm_to_camps.py:91, historical_arc.py:1677). influence.py:120-163 graded branch adds a logistic weight and an affect multiplier absent from classic HK. literature.md:85-88 ties Hegselmann-Krause to 'recovered exactly as the complete-graph special case (compass_basic, test_canonical.py)'.
why_it_matters: Under measure-then-bless, golden tests confirm outputs didn't change, not that they're correct; here the one genuinely literature-anchored convergence test isn't even on the shipped code path. The HK 'L' tag is honest for the reduction but the shipped graded+affect filter is N/E and is not independently validated against any published bounded-confidence result.
suggested_fix: Either add a test pinning the graded (temperature=0.05) path to a published adaptive-bounded-confidence result (Kan/Porter/Mason 2023 is already cited as the network-coupled precedent), or annotate that the shipped BC path is an N/E extension of HK whose literature validation (test_canonical) covers only the temperature=0 reduction, not the shipped configuration.

title: ArgumentExchange is a documented, citation-tagged rule that no shipped scenario uses, and its Mäs-Flache mapping is loose
claim: ArgumentExchange (abm/rules/argument_exchange.py) is exported and documented as 'simplified Mäs-Flache ACTB (2013)' but is instantiated by neither the pillar (calm_to_camps.py) nor the historical arc (historical_arc.py) — it is dead code in both shipped tracks. Separately, its implementation is a loose analog of ACTB: it pulls toward a neighbor's position magnitude (delta[axis]=step*neighbor.ideology[axis]) rather than adopting discrete pro/con arguments, and the docstring admits the homophily filter that produces ACTB's bi-polarization is 'vestigial post-ADR-001'.
evidence: Grep: ArgumentExchange appears only in abm/rules/__init__.py:2,15 and its own file; absent from both pillar files' rule lists. argument_exchange.py:40-48: 'homophily_radius is vestigial', picks one random neighbor and one random axis, delta = step*neighbor.ideology[axis]. literature.md:92 maps Mäs & Flache 2013 to BacklashRepulsion, not ArgumentExchange, suggesting the ACTB rule was superseded.
why_it_matters: A rule presented in the engine's public API and docstring with a specific peer-reviewed mechanism (ACTB) but unused in every shipped build inflates the apparent mechanistic richness of the model. Without the homophily filter it cannot reproduce the bi-polarization ACTB is known for, so even if activated the 'ACTB' label would be an overclaim.
suggested_fix: Either remove ArgumentExchange (it is unused) or clearly mark it as a deprecated/experimental rule not part of the canonical model, and correct or soften the Mäs-Flache mapping in its docstring.

title: ConstraintOp is faithfully implemented but its dispersion noise outweighs its constraint force, and the rate is a free fit not a literature-anchored magnitude
claim: ConstraintOp's code matches its docstring (network-local consensus projection with no party/centroid reference; the anti-centroid property holds). But at the canonical operating point (rate=0.0348, resid_sigma=0.01) the rule's dispersion counterweight (resid-noise mean||d||=0.0148) is LARGER than its actual constraint-collapse force (projection mean||d||=0.0113), and within-party effective dimensionality sits at ~2.0-2.1 of 7 — right at the documented '>2' tripwire. The 'L (DellaPosta/Boutyline)' tag covers the mechanism; the rate magnitude is an S4 ABC fit (N), honestly tagged, but is the dominant calibrated quantity actually driving constraint.
evidence: Inline probe (validation/audit, ConstraintOp isolation at tick 90): resid-only mean||d||=0.0148 > proj-only mean||d||=0.0113; Dem within-party participation ratio 2.11, Rep 2.03. constraint_op.py:78-99 matches docstring (no centroid term). Rate 0.0348 fitted at S4 (anes_preset.py:111 'was 0.02 — fitted'); methods.md §5.20 prior center 0.02 anchored to Kozlowski/Hare constraint slope but the shipped value is the ABC fit, not the literature slope.
why_it_matters: The emergent-constraint claim (blindspot #7 narrative: 'sorting now emerges') leans on ConstraintOp. That the noise term dominates the collapse term and eff-dim hovers at the tripwire means the 'constraint' is weak and largely held in check by its own dispersion — the emergent correlation growth is modest and the calibrated rate, not a literature-derived slope, sets it. Worth disclosing that the constraint force is near the boundary of being a no-op.
suggested_fix: Report the constraint-vs-dispersion magnitude balance and the within-party eff-dim at the shipped rate in methods §5.20, and make clear the shipped rate (0.0348) is a free fit whose only literature anchor is a prior center (0.02) that was then overridden by the ABC fit.

id: MECHANISM FAITHFULNESS — party / identity / affect (divergence) rules-1
stream: MECHANISM FAITHFULNESS — party / identity / affect (divergence) rules
stream_summary: The party/identity/affect divergence rules are individually plausible and literature-cited, but two faithfulness problems are serious. (1) The shipped engine systematically OVER-PRODUCES out-party animus: at the canonical config (endogenous + cultural + econ common-mode), out-party affect is out of the grounded ANES affect band at every single decade (0/5), across 3 independent seeds AND the ensemble mean — and this is visible in the project's own blessed scorecard (phase9_anes_score_anes_full.json scores affect 0/5 against its own ANES bands while reporting 4/5 against the OLD too-cold bands). The methods doc claims the affect re-grade "places all five affect decades in the data-grounded affect bands" and frames the miss as "one orthogonal pre-existing cell"; both are false for the shipped config. I traced the regression to the cultural common-mode channel (added to fix the cultural AXIS), which knocked affect from 4/5-in-band to 0/5 as an uncalibrated side-effect. (2) A large set of the audited rules (IdentitySorting, IdentityAlignment, the dyadic identity-distance affect term) do NOT run in the shipped build at all — they are replaced by ConstraintOp / MeasuredAlignment / identity_weight=0, and BacklashRepulsion ships at strength 0 — yet CLAUDE.md, methods.md, and literature.md still describe them as the shipped mechanisms, including the prompt's own "IdentityAlignment ships and emits the metric" premise. Provenance for the live affect channels (MediatedAnimus lr, identity-alignment affect weight) is honestly tagged N, but the mechanism is double-applied (identity_alignment feeds both AffectiveUpdate's align_factor and MediatedAnimus).
title: Shipped engine over-produces out-party animus: 0/5 affect decades in the grounded ANES band, contradicting the methods claim
claim: On the canonical shipped config (ANES_FULL_KWARGS = endogenous + cultural + econ common-mode), the mean out-party affect trajectory is COLDER than the grounded ANES affect band at every decade, across 3 seeds and the ensemble mean. methods.md §5.27 claims the affect re-grade 'places all five affect decades + the 1980 IC in the data-grounded affect bands'; that is false for the shipped config.
evidence: My run (validation/audit/affect_band_score.py, seeds 0/1/2): 1990 ens -0.248 vs band [-0.206,-0.096]; 2000 ens -0.437 vs [-0.313,-0.179]; 2010 ens -0.584 vs [-0.512,-0.412]; 2020 ens -0.769 vs [-0.658,-0.558]; 2025 ens -0.827 vs [-0.708,-0.508] — all 5 OUT (too cold), every seed. The model overstates how much Americans have come to dislike the out-party by ~0.1-0.13 (≈5-6 thermometer degrees) at the modern end, monotonically worsening. The methods text and the headline '17/24 ANES bands' present affect as calibrated/passing, but affect contributes ZERO of those 17 in-band cells. suggested_fix: Either re-calibrate the affect channels (AffectiveUpdate.lr / MediatedAnimus.lr / coupling) against the grounded ANES bands on the FULL canonical config (not the pre-common-mode config the re-grade was tuned on), or honestly restate methods §5.27/§5.30 to say affect is 0/5 vs grounded bands on the shipped config and stop counting it as a calibrated channel. Do not score affect against the deprecated wide bands while citing the re-grade as the justification.

title: The cultural common-mode channel silently broke the affect calibration (uncalibrated cross-axis side-effect)
claim: The cultural common-mode channel (added to correct the cultural-AXIS center of mass, calibrated only against ANES/GSS cultural position) shifted out-party affect ~0.18 colder at the modern end and is the proximate cause of affect leaving the grounded bands. The affect re-bless was never re-run after the channel was stacked on.
evidence: validation/audit/affect_band_compare_configs.py (seed 0): ENDOGENOUS pre-common-mode = 4/5 affect bands in (1990 -0.204, 2000 -0.319, 2010 -0.430, 2020 -0.591, 2025 -0.655); +CULTURAL common-mode = 0/5 (2010 -0.572, 2020 -0.775, 2025 -0.832); +ECON common-mode (canonical) still 0/5. Mechanism: CommonModeCulture shifts cultural positions -> larger issue_term in AffectiveUpdate -> more cooling. methods.md §5.30 acknowledges only 'ANES scorecard 17/24 (was 18, the one cell is the orthogonal pre-existing affect-too-cold issue)' — undercounting (it is 5 affect cells vs grounded bands, not 1) and mislabeling the cause as pre-existing/orthogonal when the channel demonstrably worsened it.
why_it_matters: This is a measure-then-bless discipline failure of exactly the kind the project warns against: a change calibrated on one axis degraded a previously-calibrated output (affect) by a large margin, and the regression was characterized as a pre-existing single cell rather than measured and re-blessed. It undermines confidence that the multi-channel canonical config was holistically validated rather than assembled axis-by-axis.
suggested_fix: Re-measure affect against the grounded bands on the full canonical config and re-bless honestly (re-tune affect lrs jointly with the common-mode channels), or document the cross-axis affect regression explicitly in §5.30 with the per-decade numbers, not as 'one orthogonal pre-existing cell'.

verification.reasoning:
I independently reproduced and extended the cited evidence; the finding holds.

(1) EMPIRICAL CORE REPRODUCES EXACTLY. Running the cited validation/audit/affect_band_compare_configs.py (seed 0) reproduces the claimed numbers to 3-4 dp: ENDOGENOUS 1990 -0.2037 / 2000 -0.3185 / 2010 -0.4300 / 2020 -0.5910 / 2025 -0.6547 (4/5 grounded bands); +CULTURAL 2010 -0.5720 / 2020 -0.7753 / 2025 -0.8320 (0/5); +ECON canonical 0/5. Matches the finding's figures.

(2) THE COOLING IS CHANNEL-CAUSED, NOT "ORTHOGONAL/PRE-EXISTING." A/B at the §11-scorecard ticks across seeds 0,1,2: adding cultural_common_mode shifts out-party affect colder by -0.065 (1990) / -0.148 (2000) / -0.177 (2010) / -0.188 (2020) / -0.179 (2025) — i.e. ~0.18 at the modern end, exactly as claimed. 2010/2020/2025 flip from comfortably in-band to out-of-band purely from the channel (2010: ENDOGENOUS -0.487 IN -> +CULTURAL -0.664 OUT). Mechanism confirmed: the cultural-position shift increases d_iss and thus issue_term in AffectiveUpdate (abm/rules/affective_update.py:292 issue_term=d_iss/radius; :332-343 disagreement->valence), and the shift is NOT perfectly rigid/sorting-invariant because rigid_common_mode_shift clips at +/-1 (abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:110), so pairwise distances do change.

(3) THE §5.30 "ONE CELL" FRAMING UNDERCOUNTS AND MISLABELS. The §11 scorecard (scripts/phase9_anes_score.py:203 metrics_5 includes "affect") has 5 affect cells (years 1990-2025) graded against the re-grounded bands (scripts/phase8f_lib.py:78-100 ANES_PRIMARY_TARGETS) that are identical to the audit's grounded affect_bands.json. My full 24-cell tally (seed 0): ENDOGENOUS 16/24 -> +CULTURAL 13/24, flipping 5 cells (1 gained: 2000:party_sep; 4 lost: 2010:affect, 2020:party_sep, 2020:affect, 2025:affect — 3 of them affect). methods.md:2019-2020 says "ANES scorecard 17/24 (was 18, the one cell is the orthogonal pre-existing affect-too-cold issue)" — that is contradicted by the project's OWN later docs: docs/results/blindspot_priority.md:88-94 ("5 AFFECT cells... too cold at EVERY wave; 5 of 9 realism out-cells; the main scorecard miss") and docs/results/realism_report.md:79 (all five 1990/2000/2010/2020/2025 affect cells OUT, 15/24). So the §5.30 affect cost was reported as 1 pre-existing cell when it is 3 channel-caused flips plus material worsening (~0.07-0.19) of the 2 already-cold cells.

(4) WORDING REFINEMENT (not a refutation): affect was already below band at 1990/2000 on ENDOGENOUS (pre-existing blindspot #1 too-cold), so the channel is the proximate cause for 3 of 5 cells and a major aggravator of the other 2, rather than the sole cause of all 5. The finding's "proximate cause of affect leaving the grounded bands" is true for the majority of cells. Also a re-MEASURE of the scorecard did occur (so "never re-run" is imprecise), but the affect was never re-CALIBRATED to compensate — the regression was deferred to blindspot #1 and labeled pre-existing/orthogonal, which is the substance of the discipline failure the finding flags.

Severity major is correct: a change calibrated on the cultural axis degraded a previously-in-band output (affect) by ~0.18 at every modern cell and the regression was under-reported (1 cell vs >=3 flips) and mis-attributed as orthogonal/pre-existing in the canonical methods doc — exactly the measure-then-bless discipline the project advertises. Not critical because the shipped numbers are internally consistent (the buckets/contract are unchanged) and the true magnitude IS honestly captured elsewhere in the repo (realism_report 15/24, blindspot #1); the failure is the specific §5.30 characterization, not a hidden corruption of outputs.

title: Audited divergence rules (IdentitySorting, IdentityAlignment, dyadic identity term, BacklashRepulsion) do NOT run in the shipped build, but docs say they do
claim: On the canonical shipped pipeline, IdentitySorting and IdentityAlignment are absent (replaced by ConstraintOp and MeasuredAlignment), AffectiveUpdate.identity_weight=0.0 and baseline=0.0 (the Mason dyadic identity-distance term and the per-encounter coolness floor are OFF), and BacklashRepulsion ships at strength=0.0. Yet CLAUDE.md, methods.md, and literature.md still describe these as the live shipped mechanisms, including the prompt's premise that 'IdentityAlignment ships and emits the identity_alignment metric.'
evidence: validation/audit/stream_affect_check.py on build_engine(0, **ANES_FULL_KWARGS): pipeline = [BC, PartyPull, FactionAnchor, MediaConsumption, AffectiveUpdate, MediatedAnimus, ConstraintOp, MeasuredAlignment, BacklashRepulsion, PerceptionUpdate, GaussianNoise, IdentityToIdeologyPull]; IdentitySorting in pipeline=False, IdentityAlignment in pipeline=False; AffectiveUpdate.identity_weight=0.0 baseline=0.0; BacklashRepulsion.strength=0.0. publish_web_data.py L284-291 reads identity_alignment from agent attrs (so the web metric comes from MeasuredAlignment, not IdentityAlignment). CLAUDE.md 'two honest caveats' + literature.md L165 list IdentityAlignment/IdentitySorting as shipped/anchored; MediatedAnimus docstring (mediated_animus.py L26) claims identity_alignment 'accelerates 0.21->0.41 over the arc' but the shipped MeasuredAlignment readout is 0.20->0.33 (my run).
why_it_matters: A reviewer (or the project's own 'isolation drift-guard' bookkeeping) reading the docs would audit and pin the wrong rules. The project's stated open caveat — 'IdentityAlignment has no isolation drift-guard' — is moot because IdentityAlignment doesn't ship; the rule that DOES ship (MeasuredAlignment) is a different mechanism (a geometric-mean readout, not a Mason relaxation stock). Doc/code drift of this size on the central identity->affect channel is a transparency problem for a self-described 'teaching artefact' whose whole value proposition is honest provenance.
suggested_fix: Update CLAUDE.md (the 'two honest caveats' + status), methods.md, and literature.md to describe the actual shipped mechanisms (ConstraintOp, MeasuredAlignment, identity_weight=0, baseline=0, BacklashRepulsion strength 0 at baseline). Fix the stale MediatedAnimus docstring 0.21->0.41 number. Move the 'missing isolation guard' note from the retired IdentityAlignment to whatever live rule actually lacks one.

verification.reasoning:
Mechanical core fully reproduced on build_engine(0, **ANES_FULL_KWARGS): pipeline = [BC, PartyPull, FactionAnchor, MediaConsumption, AffectiveUpdate, MediatedAnimus, ConstraintOp, MeasuredAlignment, BacklashRepulsion, PerceptionUpdate, GaussianNoise, IdentityToIdeologyPull]; IdentitySorting and IdentityAlignment both absent; AffectiveUpdate.identity_weight=0.0 baseline=0.0; BacklashRepulsion.strength=0.0 (hardcoded at historical_arc.py:1774-1775); shipped MeasuredAlignment readout 0.2005->0.3349(2025) vs the MediatedAnimus docstring's stale "0.21->0.41" (mediated_animus.py:26); publish_web_data.py:284-291 reads identity_alignment from agent attrs, which on this path is written by MeasuredAlignment (measured_alignment.py — a geometric-mean readout, NOT a Mason relaxation stock). So the rule-level facts are correct.

But the finding OVERREACHES on docs and conflates two different things, which is why it only partially holds:

(1) REFUTED for methods.md, the authoritative methods doc. The finding says "CLAUDE.md, methods.md, and literature.md still describe these as the live shipped mechanisms." methods.md does NOT. §5.21 (lines 1343-1399) and §5.22 (1401-1412) document the T2.4/T2.6 flip in detail and accurately: identity_weight=0.0, IdentitySorting retired/inert, IdentityAlignment replaced by a MEASURED readout, even the corrected 0.21->0.31 emergent trajectory ("vs the legacy spine's 0.21->0.41"). ENGINE_KNOBS.md:55 and 592-595 are likewise accurate ("AffectiveUpdate.identity_weight=0.0; IdentitySorting removed"). The drift the finding alleges is confined to CLAUDE.md and literature.md.

(2) BacklashRepulsion strength=0.0 is CORRECT BY DESIGN, not drift. interventions_phase6.py:97,216-252 show the historical arc deliberately initializes BacklashRepulsion at strength=0 and the X1 intervention handler turns it ON; the X1 "backfire" bucket is the X1 counterfactual vs baseline. methods.md (lines 440,447,531-534,713) correctly describes BacklashRepulsion as the X1 backfire mechanism. Listing it as a "divergence rule that does not run in the shipped build" misframes a dormant intervention lever as dead/missing machinery.

What genuinely holds and is worth fixing: CLAUDE.md:77 and 134-135 ("two honest caveats" — IdentityAlignment "ships in the web build and emits the identity_alignment metric") and literature.md:165 are factually stale for the canonical emergent build; the rule that emits the metric is MeasuredAlignment, a different mechanism. CLAUDE.md is the project guide and the prompt's own premise is drawn from it, so this is a real transparency wart. But it is doc lag in the two highest-level orientation files, contradicted by the detailed methods.md/ENGINE_KNOBS.md — not a hidden discrepancy on the central channel, and not the major-severity systemic doc/code drift the finding claims. Downgrade to minor.

title: identity_alignment is applied to affect through two parallel channels (align_factor + MediatedAnimus), partially re-introducing the 'one signal applied multiple times' problem M3-light claimed to fix
claim: MeasuredAlignment's own docstring states the alignment 'trio' (relaxation rule + AffectiveUpdate align_factor + MediatedAnimus multiplier) was 'one signal applied three times' and that M3-light reduces identity-to-affect to 'exactly ONE quantity.' On the shipped path the dyadic identity-distance term is indeed retired, but the SAME identity_alignment scalar still drives BOTH AffectiveUpdate's align_factor (weight 0.5) AND MediatedAnimus's cooling — two parallel amplifiers off one state.
evidence: affective_update.py L234-242 (align_factor = 1 + 0.5*identity_alignment, amplifies negative valence) and mediated_animus.py L81-87 (Δ = -lr*weight*identity_alignment) both read identity_alignment; my run confirms env identity_alignment_affect_weight=0.5 AND MediatedAnimus.lr=0.00917>0 simultaneously. measured_alignment.py docstring L6-9 frames the goal as collapsing this to one application.
why_it_matters: Two affect amplifiers driven by the same latent (identity_alignment) make the late-period cooling super-linear in alignment and create an identifiability hazard: the contact channel's align_factor and the parasocial channel's slope are not separately constrained by data, so the over-cooling (finding 1) cannot be cleanly attributed to a single mechanism. The 'exactly ONE quantity' framing is true for the dyadic term but not for the affect-amplification count.
suggested_fix: State explicitly in methods that identity_alignment feeds affect through two channels (align_factor and MediatedAnimus), or fold them into a single application; at minimum document that the two slopes are jointly unidentified and were not separately calibrated.

title: MediatedAnimus deliberately has NO saturation, guaranteeing the affect floor is hit late — structurally biases toward over-cooling
claim: MediatedAnimus is explicitly designed with no diminishing-returns saturation ('its whole purpose is to keep biting once the network has sorted'), relying only on the hard clip at -1. Combined with AffectiveUpdate's saturation also being retired under evidence_regrade, the only late-period brake on cooling is the +-1 clip — which structurally pushes the modern-era trajectory toward the floor, consistent with the measured over-cooling.
evidence: mediated_animus.py L32-36 ('No saturation... the metric-reader's clip at +-1 is the hard backstop'); affective_update.py saturation=0.0 under evidence_regrade (historical_arc.py L1724-1727, confirmed AffectiveUpdate.saturation=0.0 in my run). Iyengar 2019 fig.1 / Mason ch.6 (cited as the saturation anchor) describe diminishing-returns/ceiling shape — the opposite of an unsaturated channel running to the clip.
why_it_matters: The empirical out-party thermometer flattens 2020->2024 (19.0->20.4 deg per affect_bands.json), i.e. it is decelerating near the modern floor — yet the shipped channel is built to accelerate into the floor with no deceleration, and the data shows the model overshoots exactly there (2025 -0.83 vs band ceiling -0.508). The 'convexity emerges' justification produces too much convexity.
suggested_fix: Reconsider giving MediatedAnimus a soft saturation (consistent with the Iyengar/Mason ceiling it cites), or document that the unsaturated design is a known contributor to the modern-era affect overshoot quantified in finding 1.

id: MECHANISM FAITHFULNESS — network / dynamics / elite rules-1
stream: MECHANISM FAITHFULNESS — network / dynamics / elite rules
stream_summary: The network/dynamics rules (TieRewiring, ResidentialMigration, CohortReplacement, ThreatDecay, FactionAnchor, GaussianNoise, ShockRelaxation, EliteDrift) are individually faithful, well-gated as no-ops on the pillar, and internally consistent; the recent protected-agent removal preserved RNG-stream integrity (the leading rng.random() guard is intact and the canonical fingerprint is unchanged). title: Endogenous loop's realignment DIRECTION is exogenously fed from ANES, not emergent
claim: ActivistEliteCue freezes each party's amplification axis at the first tick to the 1986 ANES party-gap direction (align_u = (rep-dem) item means), not to anything the simulation generates. The loop only amplifies MAGNITUDE along a hard-coded D-left/R-right axis derived from ANES party moments. The code comment is explicit that this is 'the established D-R direction from the ANES party moments' and that without it 'some seeds fizzle, some ignite (sep variance +-0.3).' So the loop cannot discover which direction the parties sort — that answer is supplied.
evidence: abm/rules/activist_elite.py:92-131 (axis frozen from rt['align_u'], R=+axis/D=-axis); abm/core/issues.py:126-139 (align_u = frozen 1986 wave rep-dem item-mean gap, 'never re-fit from the running population'). Comment at activist_elite.py:96-99 admits anchoring is required to make the loop 'reliably IGNITE rather than bifurcate on the seed.'
why_it_matters: CLAUDE.md and methods.md present blindspot #7 as resolved — 'positional sorting now emerges from the activist->elite->mass loop instead of replaying fed centroids.' verification.reasoning:
FACTS confirmed: activist_elite.py:93-131 freezes each party's amplification axis at first tick (R=+axis/D=-axis) from rt['align_u']; issues.py:126-139 defines align_u=(rep-dem)/||rep-dem|| item means from the frozen 1986 ANES wave (verified in data/mhv/issue_loadings.json: wave=1986, party_var=VCF0301, gap rep-dem=[0.316,0.345,0.344,-0.022,0.151,0.058,0.218]), built once in build_runtime, never re-fit. The comment at activist_elite.py:96-99 does claim anchoring is needed for reliable ignition vs seed bifurcation (+-0.3 sep variance). So the loop amplifies MAGNITUDE along an exogenously-fixed direction and does not discover the orientation — the finding's mechanical description is accurate.

THESIS refuted: the finding claims this contradicts the published 'emerges' framing and that the honest claim is hidden/weaker. It is not. (1) methods.md:1780-1784 states verbatim the finding's proposed honest claim: the axis 'is fixed at the first tick to the frozen partisan-gap direction align_u... anchoring the direction as exogenous historical structure... the magnitude still emerges.' (2) model_blindspots.md:52 lists the fed single axis as an explicit residual caveat (axis over-correlates compass, corr~0.78), and #10 (:84-86) elaborates the loop amplifies along a fixed 2D direction. So it is disclosed at exactly the strength the finding demands. (3) The author's direction-check, which I independently reproduced (validation/audit/fed_axis_dependence_probe.py): re-labeling the fixed 1980 cloud along the population's OWN principal axis = 0.646 vs fed align_u = 0.644 vs economic x-axis = 0.629, despite the fed axis sitting ~10.6deg off the PC — the supplied direction coincides with the population's emergent economic cleavage, it is not an answer re-entering.

verification.notes: Scratch probe written to validation/audit/fed_axis_dependence_probe.py (read-only audit area, no engine/doc/test files touched). title: ~72% of the party_sep rise rides an ANES-fit mobilization forcing; only ~28% is the loop's own floor
claim: The 'endogenous' loop's pace is set by a 4-knob activist-mobilization schedule (mob_base/peak/backload/asym) that is an explicit parametric curve whose convexity (mob_backload) is ABC-fit to 'back-load the emergent sep into the 2010s to match ANES.' The live honesty budget shows free_flowing=0.279 / empirical_input=0.721 for party_sep — i.e. with the forcing removed, the loop generates only 28% of the observed rise. The four-cut holdout (1/3) fails the temporal cut precisely because the late acceleration 'is NOT predictable from early dynamics — it rides the exogenously-fit activist-mobilization timing.'
evidence: docs/results/honesty_budget.json:5-14 (party_sep free_flowing 0.2793 / empirical_input 0.7207); abm/pillars/historical_arc.py:246-282 (compute_activist_mobilization_schedule; mob_backload is 'the convexity ... which back-loads the emergent sep into the 2010s to match ANES'); scripts/audit/e4_fit.py:50-59 (8 knobs incl. mob_backload prior 0..2.5 fit to ANES per-decade bands); scripts/audit/e5_holdout.py:264-276.
why_it_matters: This is the difference between 'the model predicts the polarization trajectory' and 'the model is calibrated to reproduce a trajectory whose timing is fed in.' The mechanism is real but the headline result (party_sep ~1.0 by 2025) is mostly carried by a curve fit to the ANES shape. The project documents this as an open caveat, but the magnitude (72% forced) deserves to be in the top-line characterization, not only in an audit file.
suggested_fix: Lead with the budget split wherever the emergence win is claimed; characterize the loop as 'mechanism endogenous, timing calibrated' rather than 'positional sorting emerges.'

verification.reasoning: Every load-bearing claim checks out against code/data. (1) honesty_budget.json:8-9 has party_sep free_flowing 0.2793 / empirical_input 0.7207; I independently re-ran the t35_budget_brake.py decomposition (audit_lib.freeze_worker, 2 seeds) and got free_flowing 0.3508 / empirical_input 0.6492 — same magnitude (sampling spread vs the blessed 6-seed value), b1 matched 1.082 vs 1.0843. So "~72% rides the forcing, ~28% loop floor" is sound. (2) Mechanism confirmed: compute_activist_mobilization_schedule (historical_arc.py:273-282) is a convex parametric curve lvl=base+(peak-base)*tau^(1+2*backload); the in-code comment (L260-264) says mob_backload "back-loads the emergent sep into the 2010s to match ANES"; adopted mob_backload=1.3548 (anes_preset.py:175) = strong convexity. activist_elite.py:84 reads activist_mobilization, ramped ONLY by dated decade/event handlers (_set_activist_mobilization, L2389-2478); frozen → stays at low 1980 baseline = the floor. (3) ABC fit (e4_fit.py:50-59,127-134) minimizes weighted distance to ANES per-decade bands with party_sep weighted 1.0; mob_backload is a fitted knob (prior 0..2.5) — genuinely fits the trajectory shape. (4) e5_holdout.py:264-276 states the temporal cut fails because the late acceleration "is NOT predictable from early dynamics — it rides the exogenously-fit activist-mobilization timing." (5) Attribution to mobilization specifically (not events/media) is accurate: budget forcing diagnostic = -0.0424 (events ~null for party_sep); methods.md:1894 independently reports "mobilization ramp OFF" floor = 0.55-0.57 (+21-28% of rise), triangulating the budget. The finding is correct on all points.
verification.notes: Two corrections, both in the finding's favor (it understated the issue): (a) The split is NOT buried in an audit file as the finding worried — it IS top-line in docs/methods.md:1820-1823 and the public web Methods honesty panel (web_demo/cc-pages.jsx:91-96,230-254). BUT both quote the STALE 38%/62% (pre-common-mode E5.8) numbers, not the live canonical 28%/72% in the shipped honesty_budget.json. The JSON's own _provenance (L2) records the 0.38->0.28 drop driven by the cultural-fix turnover bump. So the public-facing/methods numbers understate the empirical-input share by ~10 pts relative to the config that actually ships — a documentation-staleness defect layered on top of the finding. (b) The 6-seed b0 in the JSON is 0.3427 vs my 2-seed 0.2995; free_flowing is somewhat seed-sensitive (0.28-0.35 range), so "28%" is a point estimate with spread, but the qualitative conclusion (loop floor is a minority share, ~quarter-to-third) is robust. Kept severity at major: no engine correctness bug, limitation is documented in spirit, but the emergent headline (party_sep~1.0 by 2025) is materially carried by an ANES-shape-fit forcing AND the published magnitudes are stale-low.

title: Cohort replacement leaks the prior occupant's social_coord onto the new person
claim: Both _replace_agent_inplace and _replace_independent_inplace reseed party, position, identities, affect, anchor, party_cue, media_diet, etc., but never reseed social_coord. So a replaced slot (~61% of slots over the arc at the canonical rate 0.007) keeps the previous person's social_coord — a person-level lifestyle/residential latent — even though the docstring frames replacement as 'the person changes.' combined_distance reads social_coord with raw indexing, so this inherited value feeds tie-rewiring/modularity.
evidence: abm/rules/cohort_replacement.py (no 'social_coord' assignment anywhere — grep returns no matches in the file); _replace_agent_inplace at lines 385-449 reseeds every other person-level attr; abm/core/network.py:31-34 (combined_distance uses social_coord, raw a.state.attrs['social_coord']); canonical cohort_replacement_rate=0.007 (scripts/anes_preset.py:198).
why_it_matters: social_coord is the migration/tie-formation anchor; inheriting it across a person-swap means a 'new person' is born already located at the old person's lifestyle position. It is a small, undocumented mechanism inconsistency in the network substrate, and at 0.007/tick it touches a majority of slots over the run. Likely second-order for party_sep/affect, but it muddies the 'node persists, person changes' invariant for the network metrics specifically.
suggested_fix: Either reseed social_coord from the cohort distribution on replacement (and re-bless), or document explicitly that social_coord is a node-level (not person-level) latent that intentionally survives replacement.

title: Elite leapfrog gain is large enough that the elite is governed by the saturating tanh ceiling, not the live activist tail
claim: With the adopted E4 point (elite_gain=1.769, mob_peak=2.484, mob_asym=0.188), the effective leapfrog by 2020-25 is g = gain*mob*(1+/-asym) ~5.2 for Republicans / ~3.6 for Democrats. raw = cent + g*(tail_mean - cent) then elite = ceiling*tanh(raw/ceiling) with ceiling=0.824. At those gains the tanh argument is far into saturation for most of the late period, so the elite position is effectively pinned near +-ceiling — a fixed N-tagged parameter — rather than tracking the live intensity-weighted activist tail. The 'elites track the activist tail' mechanism story (Bawn/Zaller) becomes 'elites sit at the ceiling' in the operating regime.
evidence: abm/rules/activist_elite.py:146-149 (g = gain*mob; raw = cent + g*(tail_mean-cent); elite = ceiling*tanh(raw/ceiling)); scripts/anes_preset.py:171-178 (elite_gain 1.7689, elite_ceiling 0.8237, mob_peak 2.4838, mob_asym 0.1880); methods.md:1190 notes the operating point 'sits on three saturations (elite centroids ...).'
why_it_matters: If the elite is ceiling-saturated, the literature-grounded 'activist tail -> elite' coupling is not what sets the late-period elite position — the bounding nonlinearity (an N design choice) is. That weakens the L provenance of the elite channel in the regime that actually ships, and means seed/tail variation has little influence on the late elite, consistent with the observed low free-flowing share.
suggested_fix: Report the tanh saturation fraction over the arc; if the elite is ceiling-bound for most of 2010-25, downgrade the elite-tracking-tail claim's provenance to N in that regime and document it alongside the existing saturation note.

title: "~69% cohort replacement" — the magnitude licensing the cultural common-mode channel's L/emergent tag — is a non-identified APC self-computation, and the cited literature reports 33–55%, not 69%
claim: The cultural common-mode channel (abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py) is tagged provenance L (literature-supported) and sold as 'emergent, not fed' on the strength of a claim that secular cultural liberalization is '~69% cohort replacement (GSS 1972–2024 age–period decomposition; Firebaugh & Davis 1988, Brooks & Bolzendahl 2004, Baunach 2012)' (methods.md:1990-1995; cultural_common_mode.py:15-18). But (a) the 69% is the model's OWN re-computation in validation/gss_cohort.py, not a literature value; (b) that decomposition is a single weighted OLS `trad ~ birth + year` that OMITS age (lines 56-67) — the classic age-period-cohort identification problem (year − birth = age are collinear), so all age variance is forced onto the birth/year split and the 69/31 partition is NOT identified, it is an artifact of the unstated choice to drop age; (c) the three cited papers report LOWER, different numbers: Brooks & Bolzendahl 2004 give 55% (gender attitudes), and the project's own causal doc cites Baunach for '~⅓' (≈33%) for same-sex attitudes.
evidence: validation/gss_cohort.py:55-72 (OLS X = [ones, birth, year]; no age term; cohort_part/total -> the % printed); methods.md:1990-1995 and abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:15-18 (the ~69% / Firebaugh/Brooks-Bolzendahl/Baunach citations + L tag); web: Brooks & Bolzendahl 2004 = 55% of 1985–1998 gender-attitude change from cohort replacement; project docs/polarization_causal_model.md cites Baunach 2011 ~⅓ replacement.
why_it_matters: The whole honesty selling-point of this channel (vs the econ channel, explicitly called 'a weaker honesty claim... a fed forcing') is that it is EMERGENT from a measured demographic primitive. But the primitive's headline magnitude (69%) is (i) not from the literature it credits and (ii) produced by a decomposition that the APC literature — including Firebaugh, whom they cite — treats as fundamentally non-identified without an explicit constraint. An L tag and an 'emergent' framing are overclaims; the defensible tags are N (magnitude) and at most L-in-direction.
suggested_fix: Relabel the cohort-replacement share as a model-internal, constraint-dependent estimate (state the omitted-age / APC caveat next to the 69%), not a literature value; cite the actual literature ranges (≈33–55%) honestly as bracketing, and downgrade the channel's magnitude tag to N. Optionally report the split under a second APC constraint to show its sensitivity.

verification.reasoning:
All three sub-claims verified against code/data/web.

(a) 69% is the model's OWN self-computation, not a literature value. validation/gss_cohort.py:58-71 fits the OLS and prints the split via `100*cohort_part/total`; validation/gss_cohort_gradient.json records cohort_component_z=-0.263, period_component_z=-0.1181. I reproduced 69.01%/30.99% exactly. literature.md:354-356 states it plainly: "a Firebaugh-style birth-year x survey-year decomposition attributes ~69%". The three credited papers (Firebaugh & Davis 1988, Brooks & Bolzendahl 2004, Baunach 2012) are cited as method/corroboration, not as the source of 69%.

(b) The decomposition omits age and is therefore non-identified. gss_cohort.py:25 loads `age` into the dataframe but gss_cohort.py:56 builds X = [ones, birth, year] with NO age column. Since age = year - birth, the unconstrained 3-way model is exactly collinear (singular); fitting birth+year together silently imposes age-effect = 0. The 69/31 partition is an artifact of that unstated constraint, not an identified estimate. There is no robustness variant in the repo (no age-constrained model, no Yang-Land HAPC, no intrinsic estimator) — this single weighted OLS is the entire basis. Citing Firebaugh's "linear decomposition method" actually underscores the problem: that method exists precisely because the unconstrained APC model is non-identified.

(c) The cited papers report lower, different numbers. Brooks & Bolzendahl 2004 = 55% of 1985-98 gender-attitude change from cohort replacement (independently confirmed via web). Baunach (SSM) ~1/3 = ~33% — confirmed by the project's OWN docs (literature.md:346; polarization_causal_model.md:46 "~1/3 for SSM"). 69% is ~2.07x the 1/3 figure, yet literature.md:356 calls 69% "consistent with Baunach's ~1/3" — an internal contradiction.

CORRECTION (strengthens the finding): the engine does not even implement a 69/31 split. CommonModeCulture (cultural_common_mode.py:144-159), installed with default relax=1.0 (historical_arc.py:1935-1936), snaps the cultural common mode 100% to target = mean(cultural_birth_baseline(birth)) — pure cohort gradient, no within-cohort period term anywhere. So the 69% is purely narrative justification ("cohort replacement is the dominant driver, so feeding only the gradient is defensible"), while the shipped model treats the common mode as ~100% cohort-driven. Canonical config ships it: anes_preset.py:195-238 sets cultural_common_mode=True in ANES_FULL_KWARGS.

Net: the L provenance tag on cultural_common_mode.py:32 and the "emergent, not fed" headline rest on a magnitude (69%) that is (i) the author's own non-identified self-computation, (ii) credited to papers that report 33-55%, and (iii) not actually the proportion the engine uses. Defensible tags are L-in-direction (cohort replacement is a real, literature-supported driver of cultural liberalization — the SIGN/mechanism is solid) + N for the magnitude. Severity major (not critical): the cohort-replacement mechanism itself is genuinely literature-supported and correctly signed, and the channel is gated/documented as a design choice; the overclaim is in the magnitude's provenance and the "consistent with ~1/3" framing, not in the existence of the effect.
verification.notes: Strongest defense considered and rejected: "L tags the mechanism, not the 69%." Even granting that, the docs repeatedly attach the 69% to the literature ("~69% per ... Firebaugh & Davis 1988, Brooks & Bolzendahl 2004, Baunach 2012", methods.md:1990-1991; "consistent with Baunach's ~1/3", literature.md:356), so the magnitude IS being presented as lit-grounded. The non-identification (dropped age) is a genuine methods defect independent of the citation issue. Key files: validation/gss_cohort.py:25,56-72; validation/gss_cohort_gradient.json; abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:15-18,32,144-159; docs/methods.md:1989-1994; docs/literature.md:346,354-356; docs/polarization_causal_model.md:46; scripts/anes_preset.py:195-238; abm/pillars/historical_arc.py:1935-1936.

title: Treier & Hillygus 2009 axis correlation is miscited as ~0.21; the paper's figure is ~0.30 — the benchmark the model grades its "axis over-correlation" honest-gap against is wrong
claim: The docs repeatedly state the model's compass axes 'over-correlate by 2025 (corr 0.75/0.78 / slope 0.81 vs their [Treier-Hillygus] ~0.21)' and use ~0.21 as the empirical 2D-axis correlation target (literature.md:271; methods.md:1710-1711, 1962; e5_holdout.md cut-2). The most authoritative source I found states the two Treier-Hillygus dimensions are 'only modestly correlated at 0.30', not 0.21. I could not extract the original PDF (binary) to read the exact in-paper coefficient, so I report this with medium confidence — but 0.30 is the commonly-circulated value and 0.21 appears to be a transcription/recall error.
evidence: literature.md:271 ('corr 0.75 / slope 0.81 vs their ~0.21'); methods.md:1710-1711 ('corr 0.75 vs Treier-Hillygus ~0.21'); web search result: 'The two dimensions (economic and social) are only modestly correlated at 0.30 in Treier and Hillygus (2009).' realism_battery.py:259-274 measures corr_xy/slope but the ~0.21 comparison lives only in the prose grade.
why_it_matters: This is the single quantitative benchmark for one of the model's headline self-declared blindspots (axis over-correlation, blindspot #10, the holdout cut-2 failure). Reporting the gap as 0.75-vs-0.21 overstates the miss relative to the true 0.75-vs-0.30; conversely it means the documented 'realism gap' is graded against a target the paper does not contain. The model is over-correlated either way, so the qualitative finding survives — but a load-bearing empirical number is wrong and propagates across literature.md, methods.md, and the holdout report.
suggested_fix: Verify the exact coefficient in the Treier & Hillygus PDF and correct ~0.21 -> the paper's value (~0.30) everywhere it is used as the axis-correlation target; re-state the over-correlation gap against the corrected number.

title: "Thermostatic policy mood (Erikson–MacKuen–Stimson)" is used to dignify a hand-drawn, single-scalar-fit econ forcing the source would not endorse for economic self-placement
claim: The economic common-mode channel (CommonModeEconomic) is provenance-tagged L (thermostatic common-mode mechanism) and framed as 'the thermostatic policy mood (Erikson–MacKuen–Stimson, The Macro Polity; Wlezien)' (methods.md:2046-2061; cultural_common_mode.py:173-201). But what actually ships is a 4-point hand-drawn curve (ECON_MOOD_ANCHOR_YEARS [1980,1996,2012,2024], ECON_MOOD_ANCHOR_SHAPE [0.30,1.00,0.45,-0.55]) with a single fitted amplitude (0.09) calibrated to ANES/GSS, NOT the thermostat. The docs themselves show that the real Stimson series, fed literally, FALSIFIES the thermostatic reading on this axis (it injects a spurious +0.20 econ spike at 2012; secular component wrong-signed; r≈0.38). So Stimson supplies only loose directional 'corroboration', and the actual driver is a curve tuned to reproduce the ANES target it is meant to explain.
evidence: cultural_common_mode.py:207-215 (the 4-anchor shape + ECON_MOOD_AMPLITUDE=0.09 single fitted scalar) and 246-254 (snaps partisan econ mean to the fed curve); methods.md:2065-2077 (Stimson 'fed literally injects a spurious +0.20 econ spike at 2012... falsified in validation/exp_econ_commonmode.py'); web: Macro Polity 'policy mood' is a government-activity/spending-preference index (mood moves OPPOSITE to policy), not economic ideological self-placement.
why_it_matters: An L provenance tag and a famous-citation label ('Erikson–MacKuen–Stimson thermostat') confer empirical authority on what is, by the docs' own falsification test, a curve-fit to the target — exactly the 'painting the target on' that methods.md §3's own 'dated-referent test' forbids. The inflection YEARS map to real events (admissible), but the amplitude/shape are a single-scalar fit to the ANES econ center, and the named mechanism does not transfer to the quantity being forced. To the model's credit this is partially self-flagged ('weaker honesty claim than §5.30, a fed forcing'), so the issue is the over-strong L tag + citation framing, not concealment.
suggested_fix: Downgrade the econ channel's mechanism tag from L to N (E on amplitude/shape), and reword 'thermostatic policy mood (Erikson–MacKuen–Stimson)' to 'a hand-specified mood-shaped offset whose direction is loosely corroborated by — but not derived from — Stimson policy mood; note Stimson measures spending preference, not self-placement, and is falsified if fed literally.'

title: calibration.py EMPIRICAL_ANCHORS still asserts the ANES-thermometer 'within ~5% of the ANES anchor' claim that methods.md §3.1 already retracted
claim: The auditable anchor registry (abm/calibration.py EMPIRICAL_ANCHORS['out_party_thermometer'].model_check) states the pillar affect trajectory 'projects to ~-0.53 — within ~5% of the ANES anchor.' But methods.md §3.1 (lines 178-185) explicitly walks this back: the 'within ~5%' is a 'derived projection from the full-window measurement, not a separate measurement at 126 ticks', and the 2026-06 affect re-grade further found the old bands the engine was calibrated to 'ran ~0.2 too cold' (methods.md:47, 917-945). evidence: abm/calibration.py:57-62 ('projects to ~-0.53 — within ~5% of the ANES anchor'); methods.md:178-185 (the 'within 5%' is a derived projection, not a measurement; 'guards trajectory shape at one horizon, not anchor agreement across all horizons'); methods.md:47 + 917-945 (old affect bands ran ~0.2 too cold, re-derived).

title: Verified-sound anchors (reported for completeness, no silent gaps): Pettigrew-Tropp, DW-NOMINATE, ANES thermometer, Mason
claim: Four of the load-bearing anchors verify cleanly and are honestly handled. (1) Pettigrew & Tropp 2006: mean r=-0.21 across 515 studies — cited EXACTLY (calibration.py:79, methods.md:205), and the 'r=-0.21 -> cooperative_mute=0.5' step is correctly tagged E/extrapolation (methods.md:225-233), which is the right call since a cumulative correlation is not a 50% per-encounter reduction. (2) DW-NOMINATE elite divergence: Pew/Voteview put the House party-median first-dim gap rise at ~0.33 (R 0.25->0.51, D -0.31->-0.38) over ~50yr; the model's '~0.4 units' (calibration.py:67) is slightly high but defensible across chambers/through-2020+. (3) ANES out-party thermometer ~48 deg(1978)->~20 deg(2020), -28pt: direction and magnitude confirmed against Iyengar 2019 reporting. (4) Mason 2018 mega-identity stacking -> affective polarization: accurately characterized (literature.md:163-166).
evidence: Web: Pettigrew-Tropp 2006 'mean effect size r=-.21' (515 studies, >250k subjects), experimental subset r=-.33; Pew 2022 House DW-NOMINATE R 0.25->0.51 / D -0.31->-0.38; Iyengar 2019 out-party rating ~just-below-50 in 1978 falling steadily; Mason Uncivil Agreement 'partisanship as mega-identity'. Repo: abm/calibration.py:48-92; methods.md:157-233.
why_it_matters: Establishes that the engine's empirical SPINE (influence, elite, affect, identity) is genuinely literature-grounded and, in the highest-stakes case (Pettigrew-Tropp), honestly hedged — so the weaknesses above are concentrated in the newest common-mode/forcing machinery (§5.30/§5.31) and one stale registry string, not the core. Reported so the verification record has no silent gaps.
suggested_fix: No change needed; documents the verified-clean set so the negative findings are scoped correctly.

id: FORCING LEDGER — is the fit earned or fed?-1
stream: FORCING LEDGER — is the fit earned or fed?
stream_summary: I inventoried every exogenous input to the canonical anes_full build (ANES_FULL_KWARGS = ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS in scripts/anes_preset.py:224-238) and classified each as a legitimate FORCING vs an ANSWER. The honest verdict: the model's three differential headline metrics (party_sep, identity_alignment, affect) are NOT directly fed positions anymore (the data-fed PartyCentroidSeries is off; endogenous_elite=True), and that is a genuine improvement over the pre-E5 replay. BUT the two non-differential headline results — the economic and cultural center-of-mass on each axis — are ~100% fed by construction and the project's own honesty budget explicitly does not track them. And the headline EARNED metric, party_sep, has its entire late-period magnitude/shape supplied by an activist-mobilization schedule that is ABC-fit directly to the ANES party_sep trajectory it claims to explain — the project's own four-cut holdout (docs/results/e5_holdout.md) FAILS the temporal cut specifically on party_sep, and the re-blessed honesty budget shows only ~28% of the party_sep rise is the loop's spontaneous floor (~72% rides the fitted forcing). Separately, the top-level project status (CLAUDE.md) still advertises 18/24 scorecard / 18/24 realism while the model's own re-measurement on the actual shipped config is 15/24 — a FAIL against the project's stated ≥18/24 acceptance gate, and nothing in the test suite enforces it. The lower-level docs (methods.md, realism_report.md) are candid about all of this; the overclaims live in the headline/status layer.
title: party_sep's late magnitude is fed: the activist-mobilization schedule is ABC-fit to the ANES party_sep trajectory it 'explains' (holdout FAILS on exactly this)
claim: The canonical endogenous loop's headline claim ('positional sorting EMERGES') is undercut because the exogenous activist-mobilization schedule that paces the loop is calibrated directly to the ANES party_sep curve. The 8 loop knobs — including mob_base/mob_peak/mob_backload (the convexity that 'back-loads' the rise into the 2010s) — are ABC-fit with party_sep weighted 1.0 as the dominant target (scripts/audit/e4_fit.py:62-66 OBS_W={'party_sep':1.0,...}; FIT_PARAMS lists all four mob_* knobs + uptake + fj). The shipped values are the adopted ABC point (scripts/anes_preset.py:170-178: mob_peak=2.4838, mob_backload=1.3548, etc.). The model's OWN out-of-sample test confirms the late trajectory is not predictable: the four-cut holdout (docs/results/e5_holdout.md) FAILS cut1_temporal SPECIFICALLY and ONLY on party_sep — refit on <=2000 predicts 2025 party_sep [0.502,0.698] vs needed [0.97,1.25]; affect/constraint/within_sd all pass.
evidence: scripts/audit/e4_fit.py:5-13 (fits to 'the ANES per-decade SHAPE...the 2010s acceleration + the endpoint'), :62-66 (OBS_W party_sep=1.0); scripts/anes_preset.py:170-178 (shipped mob_* point); docs/results/e5_holdout.md cut1 table (2025.party_sep pred [0.502,0.698] vs band [0.97,1.25], marked **; affect/constraint/within_sd OK); honesty_budget.json party_sep free_flowing 0.2793 / empirical_input 0.7207.
why_it_matters: The central peer-review question is whether the polarization rise is generated or replayed. Feeding *positions* was replaced by feeding the *timing/shape* via a schedule fit to the same outcome. Per the model's own holdout, ~72% of the party_sep rise rides a forcing that is not out-of-sample predictable, so the model demonstrably explains a MECHANISM (loop amplification) but not the TIMING or MAGNITUDE of US polarization — the result a reader would take as the model's main finding.
suggested_fix: State plainly at the headline/abstract level (not only in e5_holdout.md / blindspot #7 residual) that the late-period party_sep magnitude is an exogenously-calibrated forcing, not a prediction; report the temporal-holdout 2025 prediction (~0.5-0.7) alongside the fitted ~1.08 wherever the 'emergence' claim appears.

verification.reasoning: Every load-bearing claim verified against code/data, not docs-as-truth. (1) e4_fit.py:68 OBS_W={"party_sep":1.0,"affect":0.4,"within_party_sd":0.4} — party_sep is the dominant fit target (0.556 of total weight; minor: it is dominant, not sole). (2) e4_fit.py:50-59 FIT_PARAMS = 8 ABC-fit knobs incl. all four mob_* + uptake (tier_c_party_pull_strength) + fj_alpha_scale. (3) historical_arc.py:273-282 compute_activist_mobilization_schedule: p=1.0+2.0*backload; lvl=base+(peak-base)*(tau**p) — mob_backload literally is the convexity; comment L260-262 says it "back-loads the emergent sep into the 2010s to match ANES." Shipped backload=1.3548 → p≈3.71 (strongly flat-early/steep-late); peak=2.4838 near the prior ceiling 3.0. (4) anes_preset.py:166-179 ANES_FULL_ENDOGENOUS_KWARGS matches the e4_fit.md ABC-median column EXACTLY (mob_peak 2.4838, mob_backload 1.3548, mob_base 0.0779, mob_asym 0.1880, uptake 0.2532, fj 1.7797, elite_gain 1.7689, elite_ceiling 0.8237); anes_preset.py:238 ANES_FULL_KWARGS = ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS chains back through COMMONMODE/ENDOGENOUS, so these knobs ARE in the canonical shipped/web build. (5) e5_holdout.md:24 cut1_temporal: refit<=2000 predicts 2025.party_sep [0.502,0.698] vs band [0.97,1.25], marked ** FAIL; affect/constraint/within_party_sd all OK — party_sep is the ONLY metric that fails the temporal cut. The refit picks mob_peak~1.65/uptake~0.06 (low ramp fitting the flat early period) vs the shipped 2.48/0.25, exactly the dependence the finding describes. (6) honesty_budget.json:9 party_sep empirical_input=0.7207; the model's OWN script t35_budget_brake.py:5-8 calls the prior 'wholly emergent' claim 'an overclaim … the loop's pace is set by an ANES-CALIBRATED mobilization forcing, so empirical data still adjusts the result.' The e4_fit.md trajectory shows the shipped point tracing ANES party_sep almost exactly (2025 1.08 vs 1.11), i.e. the curve is fit to the outcome it 'explains.' Conclusion: the headline 'positional sorting EMERGES' is materially undercut — feeding positions was replaced by feeding the timing/shape via a schedule ABC-fit to the same party_sep curve, and ~72% of the rise rides a forcing the model's own holdout shows is not out-of-sample predictable. The model explains the MECHANISM (loop amplification) but not the TIMING/MAGNITUDE.

title: The economic center-of-mass is ~100% fed (rigid snap to a curve each tick), and the honesty budget explicitly excludes it from accounting
claim: economic_common_mode=True installs CommonModeEconomic with relax=1.0, which each tick SNAPS the partisan economic center-of-mass to an exogenous thermostatic curve economic_mood_offset(year) (abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:227-254). I verified by running the canonical config vs econ-off: at 2004 econ_com=+0.0630 vs fed curve +0.0653; at 2025 econ_com=-0.0500 vs fed -0.0495 (output ≈ input to ~3 dp). With the channel OFF the model's emergent econ center sits near 0 (-0.052 at 2004). So the entire non-zero shape of the economic center-of-mass is the fed curve. The honesty budget (docs/results/honesty_budget.json _provenance) states outright: 'The econ channel is a FED forcing ... on the econ center-of-mass, which this budget does not track.' The budget tracks only party_sep/affect/identity_alignment (all differences-of-means, which are rigid-translation-invariant), so a fed center-of-mass result is presented (realism A6 per-issue, blindspot #9 'cuts the econ-COM error 64%') with zero entry in the model's fed-vs-emergent ledger.
evidence: abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:227-254 (CommonModeEconomic, relax=1.0 snaps to economic_mood_offset), :207-215 (ECON_MOOD_ANCHOR_SHAPE + amplitude 0.09); my run validation/audit/_forcing_check.py: ECON_ON 2004 econ_com=+0.0630 / fed_mood=+0.0653, 2025 -0.0500 / -0.0495; ECON_OFF 2004 econ_com=-0.0517; honesty_budget.json _provenance ('which this budget does not track'); scripts/audit/t35_budget_brake.py:43 METRICS=('party_sep','affect','identity_alignment').
why_it_matters: A peer reviewer reading 'the model reproduces the economic policy-mood arc' (blindspot #9 RESOLVED, realism A6) would assume that is a model output. It is essentially output=input by construction. This is the 'sorting-invariant, so excluded' move under-counting fed-ness exactly as the stream suspected.
suggested_fix: Add the center-of-mass axes to the honesty budget as a separate, explicitly ~0%-emergent / ~100%-fed line, or add a one-line caveat to honesty_budget.json/realism_report that the two largest fed results (econ + cultural center-of-mass) are deliberately outside the budget and are fed by design.

verification.reasoning:
I independently reproduced the finding (did not trust the auditor's _forcing_check.py or the project docs).

MECHANISM verified at code. abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:227-254 — CommonModeEconomic.apply() computes target=economic_mood_offset(year) (a fed thermostatic curve: ECON_MOOD_ANCHOR_YEARS=[1980,1996,2012,2024], SHAPE=[0.30,1.00,0.45,-0.55], AMPLITUDE=0.09, lines 207-224) and sets delta = relax*(target - partisan_econ_mean), applied as a rigid sorting-invariant translation. It is installed in the canonical build: scripts/anes_preset.py:238 ANES_FULL_KWARGS = ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS, which sets economic_common_mode=True / amplitude=0.09 (lines 224-228); historical_arc.py:1948 constructs CommonModeEconomic(amplitude=...) with the default relax=1.0 (cultural_common_mode.py:237) => it SNAPS each tick.

NUMBERS independently reproduced (own script, build_engine seed 0, full 135-tick arc, ON vs OFF). ECON_ON: partisan econ center-of-mass tracks the fed curve with max gap 0.0079 and mean gap 0.0034 across ALL ticks. Spot values match the finding's claims essentially exactly: 1996 +0.0882 vs fed +0.0900; 2004 +0.0630 vs +0.0653; 2025 -0.0500 vs -0.0495. ECON_OFF: emergent econ COM is flat near zero and does NOT track the curve (gaps up to 0.12; 2004 = -0.0517, matching the finding). So the entire non-zero, time-varying shape of the econ COM is the fed curve — output ≈ input by construction. party_sep is ~invariant ON vs OFF at every tick (2025: 1.0654 vs 1.0560; 1996: 0.5473 vs 0.5461), confirming the channel is sorting-invariant — exactly why a differences-of-means budget cannot see it.

BUDGET EXCLUSION verified. scripts/audit/t35_budget_brake.py:43 METRICS=('party_sep','affect','identity_alignment') — all rigid-translation-invariant differentials. docs/results/honesty_budget.json _provenance states verbatim: 'The econ channel is a FED forcing ... on the econ center-of-mass, which this budget does not track.' So the most directly-fed quantity in the shipped build has zero entry in the model's fed-vs-emergent ledger, whose headline (party_sep emergent 0.28 / input 0.72) reads as a completeness claim.

TWO CORRECTIONS that bound the finding's reach (why I keep major, not critical): (1) The finding cites 'realism A6 per-issue' as presenting the fed COM as a model output. A6 (realism_report.md:83) actually measures the per-issue party GAP ('econ VCF0803 gap sim 0.38->0.82'), a differential that is sorting-invariant and genuinely emergent — NOT the center-of-mass. That citation is imprecise. (2) The fed nature is NOT hidden in the prose: blindspot #9 (model_blindspots.md:67) headlines 'RESOLVED — but via a FED forcing, not emergent' and says it 'snaps ... each tick (rigid, sorting-invariant)'; methods §5.31 and the budget _provenance say the same. The 'false impression of completeness' is therefore real only against the budget headline read in isolation, not against the adjacent disclosures.

Verdict: the substance holds — a ~100%-fed result is booked as a resolved blindspot (#9, '64% error cut'), and the honesty budget structurally omits it — but the project discloses the fed-ness more honestly than the finding implies and one cited venue (A6) is mischaracterized.
