title: Top-level project status (CLAUDE.md) advertises 18/24 scorecard & 18/24 realism; the shipped config actually measures 15/24 — a FAIL of the project's own >=18/24 gate, unenforced by any test
claim: CLAUDE.md (the project's authoritative status, line ~190) states 'the re-bless cascade ran on it (scorecard 18/24, realism 18/24, ...)'. But the model's own re-measurement on the actual shipped canonical config (cultural+econ common-mode on, turnover 0.007) is 15/24, as documented in docs/results/realism_report.md (A2: '15/24 ... FAIL >=18') and docs/methods.md:2092 ('Realism battery: 15/24'). The realism_report banner calls the 18/24 a 'stale figure ... never re-measured on the shipped config'. The project defined an acceptance gate of 'ANES scorecard >= 18/24' (methods.md:1194). So the shipped config FAILS the project's own stated acceptance criterion. Crucially this is unenforced: scripts/audit/realism_battery.py:19 is explicitly 'a REPORT, not a CI gate', so none of the ~300 tests catch that the shipped build is below the bar (consistent with the measure-then-bless caveat — golden tests confirm outputs didn't CHANGE, not that they PASS).
evidence: CLAUDE.md:190 ('scorecard 18/24, realism 18/24'); docs/results/realism_report.md:10-19 + A2 row:79 ('15/24 ... FAIL >=18 (with named misses)'); docs/methods.md:2092 ('Realism battery: 15/24'), :1194 (gate 'ANES scorecard >= 18/24'); scripts/audit/realism_battery.py:1,19 ('REPORT, not a CI gate').
why_it_matters: The headline status a reader sees first overstates fidelity (18/24 PASS) versus the model's own honest measurement (15/24 FAIL) on the very config that ships and is served to the public web demo. The discrepancy is exactly the kind of stale-favorable-number a peer review must flag, and the absence of an enforcing gate means it can silently persist.
suggested_fix: Update CLAUDE.md (and any other 18/24 references describing the CURRENT shipped config) to 15/24 with the named misses; either lower the stated acceptance gate with justification or document explicitly that the shipped config is below the gate and why that was accepted.

verification.reasoning:
Independently verified every cited fact and re-derived the number from the raw artifact rather than trusting the docs.

1) CLAUDE.md:190 literally reads "re-bless cascade ran on it (scorecard 18/24, realism 18/24, ...)". Grepping CLAUDE.md (lines 48/190/304) confirms 190 is the ONLY scorecard figure in the top-level status and there is NO adjacent stale-caveat — a reader sees 18/24, which reads as PASS of the >=18 bar.

2) Actual measured value is 15/24, confirmed from the model's own measurement artifact, not just the prose. docs/results/realism_measurement.json A2 records "n24": 15, "n_mainframe": 11, "n_ic": 4, "pass": false. I hand-counted in_band=true across the 24-cell array (1990:2, 2000:2, 2010:1, 2020:3, 2025:3, 1980:4) = 15. Matches realism_report.md A2 (line 79 "15/24 ... FAIL >=18") and methods.md:2092 ("Realism battery: 15/24").

3) >=18/24 is the project's own bar. scripts/phase9_anes_score.py:319,322 hardcode "'PASS' if n>=18 else 'FAIL' (>=18)" and emit pass_18_of_24 (lines 369/376); methods.md:1194 states the user-adopted gate "ANES scorecard >= 18/24". So 15/24 is a FAIL of the project's own stated criterion.

4) Unenforced. scripts/audit/realism_battery.py:19 is explicitly "This is a REPORT, not a CI gate"; the §11 scorecard is produced by print/JSON scripts (phase9_anes_score.py, realism_battery.py), not pytest. Grepping tests/ for any 24-cell aggregation or >=18 assertion (n24, /24, >=18, sum in_band, scorecard) returns only a comment at test_t22_issues_live.py:55 — no test gates the scorecard total. The 21 test files mentioning "band" check individual pillar/arc band cells, not the aggregate scorecard pass/fail.

Net: the headline top-level status overstates fidelity (18/24 PASS) vs the model's own honest measurement (15/24 FAIL) on the exact config served to the public web demo, with no enforcing test. The finding holds exactly as stated.
verification.notes:
Severity confirmed at major (claimed), not raised to critical: this is a documentation/reporting-honesty defect, not an engine-correctness or data-integrity bug, and the honest 15/24 is recoverable two docs deep (realism_report.md banner, methods.md §5.31). Major is correctly calibrated because it is a stale-favorable number in the AUTHORITATIVE top-level status that inverts a PASS/FAIL verdict, with no gate to stop it persisting.

Caveat that mildly nuances (but does not weaken) the finding: the docs carry THREE different favorable scorecard numbers besides the honest 15 — methods.md:2087 says "17/24" (econ-flip context), and the realism_report E5.8 body (lines 62-64) still narrates "21/24 -> 18/24 (still PASS >=18)". So the precise claim "advertises 18/24" is true specifically of CLAUDE.md:190; the broader pattern (scattered, unreconciled favorable figures: 17/18/21 vs honest 15) actually strengthens the underlying concern. title: Blindspot #7 'RESOLVED' headline says party_sep emergent 1.00 while the honest budget says 0.28 — conflating loop_attributable with free_flowing
claim: docs/model_blindspots.md:51 (the #7 RESOLVED bullet) states 'party_sep emergent 1.00 (loop-attributable ...), identity_alignment 0.95, both ~0% input-carried'. This uses loop_attributable (the share that collapses if the WHOLE loop is frozen) as if it were the emergent share. The re-blessed honesty budget reports free_flowing/empirical_input of 0.28/0.72 for party_sep (honesty_budget.json), and the same doc's own residual caveat (:52-53) concedes 'only ~38% of party_sep is the spontaneous loop floor (~62% rides the fitted forcing)'. loop_attributable=1.00 only means PartyPull (which reads the cue the loop sets) is the proximate writer — it does NOT mean the rise is endogenous, because the loop's pace is the fed mobilization schedule. Quoting 1.00 'emergent' in the RESOLVED headline overstates; the honest number is 0.28-0.38.
evidence: docs/model_blindspots.md:51 ('party_sep emergent 1.00 (loop-attributable...)'), vs :52-53 ('~38% of party_sep is the spontaneous loop floor; ~62% rides the fitted forcing'); honesty_budget.json party_sep free_flowing 0.2793, loop_attributable 1.0587; scripts/audit/t35_budget_brake.py:84-86 (loop_attributable is a diagnostic 'NOT the empirical split').
why_it_matters: The single most important corrected blindspot (positional sorting was input-carried) is headlined with the metric (1.00) that most flatters the fix, while the metric that answers the actual question (0.28-0.38 free-flowing) sits two bullets down. A reader scanning the RESOLVED line takes away 'fully emergent now,' the opposite of the holdout's verdict.
suggested_fix: In the #7 RESOLVED headline lead with free_flowing ~0.28-0.38 (with the 0.72 forcing share), and reserve loop_attributable=1.00 for its diagnostic meaning ('the loop is the proximate channel') with that caveat attached.

verification.reasoning: Verified directly against code, json, and docs. (1) docs/model_blindspots.md:51 reads "party_sep emergent 1.00 (loop-attributable — freezing the loop collapses sep to the 1980 seed), identity_alignment 0.95, both ~0% input-carried." (2) The two quantities are mathematically distinct in scripts/audit/t35_budget_brake.py: free_flowing=(all_frozen−b0)/rise (line 92) vs loop_attributable=(b1−loop_frozen)/rise (line 96). The 1.00 is loop_attributable=1.0587 (honesty_budget.json:12); the metric that answers "is the rise endogenous?" is free_flowing=0.2793 (json:8). The script's own comment (t35_budget_brake.py:85-86) states loop_attributable "shows the loop is necessary, NOT the empirical split." So the auditor's core reading is correct: 1.00 means the loop is the proximate writer, not that the rise is free of the fed mobilization forcing. (3) Self-contradiction confirmed: line 51 says "both ~0% input-carried" yet the cited json reports empirical_input=0.7207 for party_sep and 0.7164 for identity (json:9,29). "~0% input-carried" is true only of the retired fed_positions channel (−0.1721 ≈ 0), not total empirical input. Line 52, two bullets later, concedes "~62% rides the fitted forcing." (4) The project's own methods.md §5.29 (lines 1813-1823) handles this honestly — it explicitly says the panel does NOT claim "wholly emergent / 0 empirical input" and reports free-flowing 0.38 / empirical 0.62 — so the blindspots headline is the weaker, overclaiming surface relative to the author's own methods text.
verification.notes: Two mitigations and one aggravation. MITIGATION: line 51 does carry a parenthetical "(loop-attributable — ...)" qualifier, so the 1.00 is not entirely naked; and the honest split exists elsewhere (line 52, methods §5.29). This keeps it at major, not critical. AGGRAVATION (found independently): the corrective number the auditor cites — "~38% spontaneous loop floor" (blindspots:52) and methods:1820 ("free-flowing 0.38") — is itself STALE. The currently re-blessed honesty_budget.json reports free_flowing=0.2793 (~28%), and the json's own provenance note (json:2) states "party_sep free_flowing fell 0.38->0.28" after the cultural-fix turnover raise. So the true free-flowing floor is ~28%, not 38% — the headline overstatement (1.00 vs ~0.28) is even larger than the auditor alleges, and the doc's own "honest" 38% is ~10 points stale. Recommend: change line 51 to lead with free_flowing≈0.28 (not 1.00), drop "~0% input-carried" (cited json says 0.72), and reconcile the stale 0.38 across blindspots:52 and methods:1820 with the json's 0.28.

title: The cultural center-of-mass 'emergent' claim rests on an ANES-measured gradient plus a turnover rate tuned to land the level
claim: The cultural common-mode channel is presented as the EMERGENT counterpart to the fed econ channel (blindspot #8 'RESOLVED ... declines emergently'), because m(t) is the mean of a per-cohort baseline times turnover. But (a) the per-cohort baseline IS a directly ANES-measured level gradient (CULTURAL_BIRTH_GRADIENT, 'Measured ANES cultural mean by birth decade', abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:42-49), and (b) the turnover rate that scales how fast the level moves is itself set to land the target (cohort_replacement_rate=0.007 in scripts/anes_preset.py:198, described as chosen so 'm(t) move[s] enough'; historical default is 0.003). So the cultural center's LEVEL SHAPE is anchored to ANES and its PACE is a tuned scalar — 'emergent' is true only in the weak sense of gradient×turnover arithmetic, not in the sense of arising from agent interaction. It is a milder version of the econ fed forcing, and the same exclusion from the honesty budget applies.
evidence: abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:42-49 (CULTURAL_BIRTH_GRADIENT 'Measured ANES cultural mean by birth decade'), :136-159 (CommonModeCulture relax=1.0 snaps to mean baseline); scripts/anes_preset.py:191-198 (turnover 0.007 'needs demographically realistic turnover ... for m(t) to move enough'); honesty_budget.json _provenance (common-mode channels 'do not enter party_sep/affect/identity_alignment' — i.e. cultural-COM also untracked).
why_it_matters: The fed-vs-emergent contrast between #8 (emergent) and #9 (fed) is doing rhetorical work — it makes the cultural fix look principled and only the econ fix 'weak.' In substance both feed an ANES-anchored center-of-mass level; the cultural one just routes it through a demographic primitive whose scaling is still tuned. A reviewer should treat both center-of-mass results as substantially fed.
suggested_fix: Soften the #8 'emergent' framing to 'semi-emergent (ANES-measured cohort gradient + a turnover rate calibrated to the target level)'; note the cultural-COM is also outside the honesty budget.

claim: data_fed_media (data/mhv/media_penetration_series.json) is a defensible FORCING: real Pew adoption fractions + documented FD-1987/Fox-1996 regime-step dates (tag L). title: Circular calibrate-and-score: one ANES recode tunes the knobs AND defines the bands AND the fit target
claim: The model's parameters and the criteria it is scored against are both derived from the same single artefact, data/phase9_empirical/derived/respondent_coordinates.csv, so the reported 'fit' is largely tautological. (1) phase9_anes_knob_anchors.py reads that CSV and emits the per-decade ANES party centroids, within-party SDs, sign-alignment, and centroid velocities that historical_arc.py consumes as PARTY_CENTERS_1980_ANES, PARTY_CUE_SIGMA_HISTORICAL_ANES, ELITE_DRIFT_SCHEDULE_ANES/_Y, and ELITE_DRIFT_ASYMMETRIC_ANES (the build_engine anes_knobs path, lines ~905-965). (2) phase8f_lib.ANES_PRIMARY_TARGETS is, by its own comment, 'Derived directly from real ANES 1986-2024 per-decade statistics in respondent_coordinates.csv' (party_sep 0.394->1.111, the very centroid distances the elite schedule was set to reproduce). (3) phase9_anes_target_builder.py weight-resamples the same CSV into the KDE pointclouds that score_engine_run Wasserstein-scores against. So the elite-drift schedule is set to walk the party centroids to the ANES per-decade positions, then the model is congratulated for landing the party centroids in a band centered on those same ANES positions.
evidence: scripts/phase9_anes_knob_anchors.py:27 (CSV path), :142-171 (centroids+velocities emitted), :238-241 (dumped to phase9_anes_knob_anchors.json); abm/pillars/historical_arc.py:905-965 (anes_knobs_active swaps in PARTY_CENTERS_1980_ANES / PARTY_CUE_SIGMA_HISTORICAL_ANES / ELITE_DRIFT_SCHEDULE_ANES); scripts/phase8f_lib.py:40-50,78-100 ('Derived directly from real ANES ... respondent_coordinates.csv', party_sep 0.394..1.111 table); scripts/phase9_anes_target_builder.py:50,80-100 (same CSV -> KDE targets); scripts/phase9_anes_score.py:153,271-278,307-315 (scorer pulls ANES_FULL_KWARGS, scores Wasserstein vs data/phase9_empirical AND §11 vs use_anes_bands=True).
why_it_matters: Under this discipline the band scorecard cannot falsify the model: any error in the recode propagates identically into the knobs and the target, so they cancel. A high in-band tally demonstrates the calibration loop closed, not that the model reproduces an independent fact about US polarization. Every headline fidelity number sourced from the §11 cells or w2_total inherits this circularity and should be relabeled goodness-of-fit, with the genuinely held-out checks (GSS A5, Pew B1) carrying the validation weight.
suggested_fix: Either (a) hold out: fit knobs on a subset of waves/decades and score on the complement, or fit on ANES and score the SAME quantities only on an independent survey (GSS), or (b) stop reporting in-band tallies as fidelity and present them explicitly as recovery/consistency checks. At minimum, never compute the scoring bands from the identical recode that set the knobs.

(a) TARGETS from the CSV: scripts/phase9_anes_target_builder.py:50,72-100 weight-resamples the CSV into per-decade KDE/pointcloud .npy files in data/phase9_empirical; scripts/phase8f_lib.py:40-50,78-100 derives ANES_PRIMARY_TARGETS (party_sep 0.394->1.111) "directly from real ANES 1986-2024 ... respondent_coordinates.csv".

(b) SCORER uses both: scripts/phase9_anes_score.py:153 sets PRESETS["anes_full"]=dict(ANES_FULL_KWARGS); :271-278 Wasserstein-scores engine snapshots vs data/phase9_empirical (CSV KDEs); :307-315 scores §11 cells vs get_primary_targets(use_anes_bands=True) (the CSV bands). Same CSV on both sides.

(c) PARAMETERS fit to those same bands, ON THE CANONICAL ENDOGENOUS PATH: scripts/audit/s4_targets.py:45-48,73-76,98-115 imports ANES_PRIMARY_TARGETS/ANES_INITIAL_TARGETS_1980 from phase8f_lib and emits per-decade mid-band points; scripts/audit/e4_fit.py:4-16,112-127 ABC-fits the endogenous loop knobs (mob_base/peak/backload/asym, elite_gain, party_pull, fj_alpha_scale) to "the ANES per-decade band mid-points" via s4_targets.build_targets(). So: knobs are tuned to land in bands centered on the same ANES stats, then the model is scored in-band -> tautological for the §11/w2 cells, exactly as claimed. The project's OWN docs/results/e5_holdout.md:51-68 admits it: "The FED config passed this cut TRIVIALLY: it fed the ANES centroid series ... the late acceleration is NOT predictable ... ~62% depends on the fitted forcing."

SEVERITY DOWNGRADED critical->major for two reasons. (1) The finding's stated mechanism is partly STALE: it pins the circularity on ELITE_DRIFT_SCHEDULE_ANES / PartyCentroidSeries "walking centroids to ANES positions" (historical_arc.py:905-965), but that is the PRE-E5 FED config (ANES_FULL_FED_KWARGS, data_fed_elite=True). The canonical shipped build is ENDOGENOUS (anes_preset.py:168-169 data_fed_elite=False, endogenous_elite=True; historical_arc.py:1829-1837 uses ActivistEliteCue, NOT the fed series or the ANES elite schedule). The circularity STILL HOLDS on the canonical path, but through the ABC-fit-to-bands channel above, not the cited fed-schedule channel. Also the 1980 seed centroids PARTY_CENTERS_PRE_REAGAN_ANES (historical_arc.py:133-139) are hand-adjusted "pre-Reagan" estimates, not the raw CSV 1980 bucket. (2) The §11/w2 scorecard is NOT the model's only check: genuinely held-out cuts exist (GSS instrument cut2, temporal cut1, s4_targets.py:127-166) and the canonical config FAILS them 1/3 (e5_holdout.md:5-9) — and that failure is documented, not hidden. So "the band scorecard cannot falsify the model" is correct for the §11/w2 cells specifically, but the broader validation is not entirely circular. Bottom line: every §11-cell / w2_total fidelity number should be relabeled goodness-of-fit, with the held-out GSS/temporal cuts (which the model partly fails) carrying the validation weight — which is the finding's own why_it_matters, and is the correct relabeling.

title: The 'independent from-raw' validation harness re-uses the calibration recode (reproduces it to 5e-05)
claim: validation/anes_from_raw.py is presented as an independent re-derivation ('Independent of abm/ and scripts/ ... so the result does not depend on the existing pipeline'), but it hardcodes the identical recode recipe as the calibration pipeline: the same 7 VCF items (VCF0803/0809/0839/0838/0830/0852/0853), the same theoretical lo/hi endpoints, the same reverse flags, and the same listwise rule. Its docstring even says it re-implements the recipe documented in scaling_params.json. I ran its cross-check: max |diff| between the from-raw centroids and the derived party_centroids.csv across 42 cells is 5e-05. That is not independent corroboration — it is a re-execution of the same construct, which can only catch a transcription bug, not a construct-validity error (e.g. whether placing the lib-cons self-placement item VCF0803 on the ECONOMIC axis, or merging racial item VCF0830 into the cultural axis, is defensible).
evidence: validation/anes_from_raw.py:1-9 (claims independence), :21-31 (ITEMS dict identical to scripts/anes_2d_compass.py:57-83), :158-177 (explicit cross-check vs derived party_centroids.csv); measured max|diff|=5e-05 over 42 year x party cells (recomputed from validation/anchors_anes.json vs derived/party_centroids.csv).
why_it_matters: A reviewer reading 'independent from-raw cross-check passed' would credit the model with external corroboration of its coordinate system. In fact the only thing verified is that the committed CSV was produced by the recipe it claims — a reproducibility check mislabeled as a validity check. Any systematic recode choice (axis assignment, item selection, direction) that biases the engine's targets is invisible to it because it shares those choices.
suggested_fix: Relabel anes_from_raw.py as a reproducibility/transcription check, not independent validation. For real construct-independence, vary the recipe (different item set, IRT/factor scores vs equal-weight means, different axis assignment of VCF0803) and show the polarization trajectory is robust; or lean validation on the genuinely different-instrument GSS path.

verification.reasoning:
Every technical claim checks out against the code/data.

1. Identical recode recipe. validation/anes_from_raw.py:21-31 defines ITEMS with the same 7 VCF items, axis assignments, lo/hi endpoints, and reverse flags as the calibration target generator scripts/anes_2d_compass.py:57-83 (ECON_CORE/CULT_CORE :82-83) and as the pipeline's persisted recipe data/phase9_empirical/derived/scaling_params.json. I diffed all three programmatically: VCF0803/0809/0839->econ (rev F/F/T), VCF0838/0830/0852/0853->cult (rev T/F/F/T), endpoints (1,7)/(1,4)/(1,5) all byte-identical. recode_item (anes_from_raw.py:42-48) is the same affine map as rescale (anes_2d_compass.py:99-105); listwise-all-core rule matches (:88 vs :226); 1-3/4/5-7 party collapse matches (:92-94 vs :108-113).

2. The 5e-05 cross-check is a re-execution, not corroboration. I recomputed it: 42 year x party cells over 14 waves, max|diff| = 4.998e-05 (econ) / 4.974e-05 (cult), mean ~2.5e-05. That residual is exactly the rounding floor (anchors_anes.json rounds centroids to 4 decimals via round(...,4) at anes_from_raw.py:115-116; half of 1e-4 = 5e-5). The two paths compute the same number; the cross-check can only catch a transcription bug.

3. Construct error is genuinely invisible AND the choices are debatable. battery.py:14-18 loads anchors_anes.json as 'reality' (PC/CORR/THERM/QUAD) and grades the engine output against it in every fact F0-F9. There is no axis-assignment or item-selection test in the battery; F1/F2/F5 presuppose the very mappings under question. The two specific concerns are real: the ANES methodology doc (data/phase9_empirical/raw/anes_2d_ideology_methodology.md:34) calls VCF0803 the 'master ideology item' (general lib-cons self-placement), yet both recodes force it purely onto ECONOMIC; and VCF0830 is documented as a RACIAL policy item (:42 'aid to minorities'; docs/internal/s2_spec.md:21-22 treats 'racial: VCF0830' as a block distinct from 'cultural-moral'), yet both recodes fold it into CULTURAL. Either choice biases the engine's targets and the 'reality' benchmark identically -> uncatchable by this harness.

WHERE THE CLAIM SLIGHTLY OVERSHOOTS (why I considered downgrading but did not): the harness's own README does NOT make the overclaim the finding implies a reviewer would credit. validation/README.md:1-23 scopes 'independent' precisely to 'does not import abm/ or tests/; reads engine output seed_0.json as an opaque file', and Design principle 2 (:20-23) explicitly frames the cross-check as 'validate both' (a reproducibility/faithfulness check), not construct validity. The author is demonstrably aware of the distinction: docs/results/realism_report.md:30-31 separates Tier A (calibrated-on, 'goodness-of-fit, not independent confirmation') from Tier B (independent), and docs/model_blindspots.md:58 describes this harness as one that 'reproduces the ANES derivation exactly, so the anchor is trustworthy' -- i.e. it markets the 5e-05 match as anchor-trustworthiness, not as external validity.

The genuine overclaim is narrower but real: the anes_from_raw.py docstring (:1-9) says 'Independent of abm/ and scripts/ ... so the result does not depend on the existing pipeline.' The phrase 'does not depend on the existing pipeline' is misleading -- the code path is independent but the result depends entirely on the same shared recode recipe (which the docstring itself admits it re-implements from scaling_params.json). REPORT.md:3 likewise leads with 'recomputed from raw, validated against the derived pipeline (max diff 0.0000)' without flagging that this validates only reproduction, not the construct.

Net: the finding's factual core (identical recipe, 5e-05 = re-execution, shared axis choices invisible to the harness, and those choices are non-trivial/debatable) is fully confirmed. Its framing that a reviewer 'would credit external corroboration' is somewhat overstated relative to the careful in-repo language, but the docstring-level overclaim it cites is genuine and the substantive risk (a systematic recode/axis choice silently biasing both targets and benchmark) is real and material to calibration validity. Severity major stands.

title: Engine 'CULTURAL_BIRTH_GRADIENT' is mis-attributed to ANES; it is a GSS z-product, and its provenance comment points at a script that reads no cohort data
claim: abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py defines CULTURAL_BIRTH_GRADIENT (born-1900 +0.169 .. born-2010 -0.340) with the comment 'Measured ANES cultural mean by birth decade, compass units ... validation/anes_from_raw.py'. But validation/anes_from_raw.py reads no birth-year, cohort, or age column at all (its columns are the 7 ideology items + party + weight + two thermometers). The actual cohort-gradient derivation is validation/gss_cohort.py, which reads GSS 'cohort'/'age' and produces a z-scored traditionalism gradient from 5 GSS moral items (homosex, premarsx, abany, fefam, grass). So the values feeding the shipped cultural common-mode channel are GSS z-units relabeled as 'ANES ... compass units', with the wrong source script cited. Whether GSS-z maps onto the engine's [-1,1] compass at the stated magnitudes (e.g. +0.169) is an unjustified unit conversion.
evidence: abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:42-49 (gradient + 'Measured ANES ... validation/anes_from_raw.py'); validation/anes_from_raw.py:79 (cols = 7 items + YEAR/PARTY/WEIGHT + THERM_D/THERM_R only — no cohort/age); validation/gss_cohort.py:16,25,31,36-49 (GSS items, reads 'cohort'/'age', emits z-per-decade gradient to gss_cohort_gradient.json).
why_it_matters: The cultural channel is the one mechanism the project markets as 'emergent, not fed' and 'L-tagged' with the strongest honesty claim. If its driving gradient is actually a GSS z-index with a mis-cited source and an unstated z->compass unit map, the provenance tag and the 'measured ANES' label overstate the empirical grounding and obscure a free scaling choice in what is presented as a measured primitive.
suggested_fix: Correct the comment to cite validation/gss_cohort.py and GSS, state explicitly that the gradient is a GSS z-index, and document/justify the z->compass-unit conversion (or refit the gradient in ANES compass units if ANES is the intended source).

verification.reasoning:
The finding's load-bearing claim — that CULTURAL_BIRTH_GRADIENT "is a GSS z-product" relabeled as ANES — is FACTUALLY REFUTED by the numbers. The engine gradient (abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:45-49) is {1900:0.169, 1910:0.165, 1920:0.142 ... 1990:-0.201, 2000:-0.288, 2010:-0.340}. The actual GSS z-product (validation/gss_cohort_gradient.json, produced by gss_cohort.py) is {1900:0.631, 1910:0.506, 1920:0.427 ... 1980:-0.297, 1990:-0.458}. These are different numbers, with GSS ~3-4x larger (raw z-scores). They are NOT the same series with a unit relabel. GSS is used in this codebase only for the 69%/31% cohort-vs-period decomposition (gss_cohort.py:66-72; literature.md:351-360; FIX_INVESTIGATION.md:11-12), NOT as the fed gradient. The engine values appear verbatim in only two files: cultural_common_mode.py:45-49 and validation/exp_demography.py:12-14 — both labeled "measured ANES cultural mean by birth decade". So the specific accusation (GSS z-units mislabeled as ANES compass units, with an unjustified z->compass conversion) does not hold; the z->compass critique is moot because the source is not GSS-z.

WHAT DOES HOLD (one sub-claim, plus a related but different defect): (1) The provenance comment IS mis-cited. cultural_common_mode.py:42-43 cites "validation/anes_from_raw.py" as the derivation, but that script (anes_from_raw.py:79) reads only the 7 ideology items + VCF0004/0301/0009z (YEAR/PARTY/WEIGHT) + VCF0218/0224 (two thermometers) — no birth-year, cohort, or age column. A by-birth-decade gradient cannot come from that script. CONFIRMED. (2) Beyond the broken citation, NO script anywhere in the repo computes the 0.169...-0.340 ANES gradient: a repo-wide grep for the ANES age/cohort columns (VCF0101/0102/0103) returns zero source hits, even though those columns DO exist in the raw file (data/phase9_empirical/raw/timeseries_csv.csv, 1030 cols). The numbers are hardcoded literals with a "measured ANES" label but no reproducible derivation. That is a real unverifiable-provenance defect — but it is NOT the leakage/mislabeling story the finding tells.

CONTEXT CHECK (the "why it matters" premise is accurate): the channel ships and is marketed as emergent. ANES_FULL_KWARGS = ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS, which sets cultural_common_mode:True (scripts/anes_preset.py:195-238; test_cultural_common_mode.py:53). The gradient drives the channel: CommonModeCulture.apply computes target = mean(cultural_birth_baseline(b)) over CULTURAL_BIRTH_GRADIENT (cultural_common_mode.py:147-159). So the gradient is load-bearing in the shipped build. However, FIX_INVESTIGATION.md H1 (lines 18-29) reports that swapping in the "measured ANES" gradient values vs the baseline produced essentially NO change in the mid-period cultural error (baseline -0.176 vs anes_gradient -0.175), because the population-mean baseline (driven by the birth-year DISTRIBUTION and turnover) is what moves m(t), not the gradient's absolute calibration — which further reduces the practical impact of the gradient's exact provenance.

NET: the finding is mostly wrong on its central, severity-justifying claim (GSS-leakage / unit-conversion) but right on a smaller point (a broken citation + a "measured ANES" primitive with no reproducible derivation). I mark it PARTIAL and downgrade major -> minor: it is a provenance/documentation-honesty defect, not a data-leakage or mislabeled-units defect.

title: The model's '1980' baseline is built from post-Reagan 1986-1988 ANES waves, then hand-overridden
claim: The recode window is 1986-2024 (1980/1982/1984 dropped to keep the VCF0852/0853 moral battery), so the empirical decade bucket labeled '1980' is actually the 1986+1988 waves, centered 1987 in the scorer (DECADE_TICKS (1980,21)=1987). The engine's tick 0 is January 1980. historical_arc.py acknowledges the 1986-88 centroids are 'POST-Reagan-shift values' and therefore does NOT use them for the seed; it substitutes hand-set PARTY_CENTERS_PRE_REAGAN_ANES. So the single most consequential anchor — the initial condition the whole path-dependent arc unwinds from — is not measured from 1980 data (none is in the window) but author-chosen, while still being scored against an 'ANES 1980' band that is really 1987 data.
evidence: data/phase9_empirical/raw/anes_2d_ideology_methodology.md:144 (window 1986-2024, 1980/82/84 dropped); scripts/phase9_anes_score.py:56-62 (DECADE_TICKS (1980,21) ANES centre 1987); abm/pillars/historical_arc.py:119-139 ('PARTY_CENTERS_1980_ANES used the ANES 1986-88 measured centroids, but those are POST-Reagan-shift values' -> PARTY_CENTERS_PRE_REAGAN_ANES alias).
why_it_matters: Initial conditions dominate a path-dependent ABM. Calling the seed 'ANES-anchored 1980' while it is (a) author-set and (b) compared to 1987 data conflates a modeling choice with a measurement and makes the early-period fit appear data-grounded when the relevant data does not exist in the build window.
suggested_fix: Relabel the bucket as ~1987, state plainly that the 1980 seed is a hand-set pre-Reagan prior (not measured), and either extend the recode to the 1980-1984 ANES waves (accepting the moral-item loss) or drop claims of 1980 empirical anchoring.

verification.reasoning:
The finding has a TRUE half and a FALSE/overclaimed half.

TRUE (verified):
1. Recode window is 1986-2024; 1980/82/84 are dropped to keep the moral battery (VCF0852/0853). Confirmed at data/phase9_empirical/raw/anes_2d_ideology_methodology.md:144.
2. The empirical "1980" decade bucket = ANES 1986+1988 waves only (n_anes_source=1719), centered 1987. Confirmed at data/phase9_empirical/phase9_empirical_build_anes.json:4-44 and scripts/phase9_anes_score.py:57 (DECADE_TICKS (1980,21) "centre 1987").
3. The tick-0 seed is author-chosen, NOT measured from 1980 data (none exists in window). historical_arc.py:119-139 sets PARTY_CENTERS_PRE_REAGAN_ANES = {D:[-0.05,+0.05], R:[+0.18,+0.10]} with literature hand-waving (Carmines-Stimson, Mason), and aliases it as PARTY_CENTERS_1980_ANES (line 139). The IC at tick 0 is built from these centers (historical_arc.py:1073-1096, anes_knobs_active path at 905-908). So the single most consequential anchor is indeed an author choice, not a measurement.

FALSE / overclaimed (the load-bearing error):
The claim's punchline — the author-set seed "is still being scored against an 'ANES 1980' band that is really 1987 data" — misreads the scoring harness. The tick-0 seed is NOT compared to the 1987 band. The scorer explicitly runs the engine to tick 21 (= 7 simulated years, 1980->1987) BEFORE sampling the "1980" snapshot, for BOTH the W2 cloud score and the §11 IC score: DECADE_TICKS and SECTION11_TICKS both map year 1980 -> tick 21 (phase9_anes_score.py:56-66), and _worker runs run_to(eng, sched, tick) for every tick>0 before snapshotting (phase9_anes_score.py:177-196; comment line 177-178: "every snapshot runs to its ANES-bucket-centroid tick before sampling — no special-case for the 1980 panel"). historical_arc.py:125-128 documents this as the *fix* for exactly the temporal mismatch the auditor alleges (item (b): "snapshotting at ticks 21/42/72/102/126 to align with the ANES bucket centroids (1987/...)"). So tick-21/1987 vs 1987-data is temporally CONSISTENT, not a conflation.

Corroboration that the scored "1980 baseline" is the evolved state, not the seed: methods.md:1891 reports engine "1980 baseline (as built)" party_sep = 0.36 (in the ANES IC band 0.33-0.47, phase8f_lib.py:101-104). The raw tick-0 PRE_REAGAN seed centroid gap is only ||[0.23,0.05]|| ≈ 0.235 — well below 0.36 — so the 0.36 figure is the tick-21 evolved snapshot, confirming the band grades evolved state.

On the "appears data-grounded" worry: the provenance discipline already flags this. methods.md:1540 explicitly tags "the 1980 anchor **E**" (extrapolated, not measured) and methods.md:1532 calls it "a pre-Reagan 1980 anchor." So the model does NOT present the seed as a measurement; it labels it as an author extrapolation.

Net: the real, defensible kernel is that the initial condition of a path-dependent ABM is an unidentified author degree of freedom with no 1980 data to pin it (a legitimate identifiability/IC concern). But the finding's framing — that a tick-0 author seed is dishonestly graded against 1987 data while masquerading as measured — does not hold: the harness scores the evolved tick-21 state against the 1987 bucket, and the provenance tag already marks the 1980 anchor as E.
verification.notes: Severity downgraded major -> minor. The factual sub-claims (window, 1987 bucket, author-set seed) are correct, but the central indictment (seed scored against 1987 band; fit made to look data-grounded) is refuted by the tick-21 alignment in the scorer and the existing "E" provenance tag. The residual valid concern is narrower and softer: the IC is an unidentified free parameter (no 1980 data), so early-period agreement partly reflects an author choice tuned so tick-21 lands in band — worth noting as an IC-identifiability caveat, not as data leakage/mislabeling. title: Thin and form-split waves carry whole decade buckets; 51% listwise retention biases the target population — uncertainty not propagated into fit claims
claim: Listwise drop on all 7 core items retains 22,761 of 44,308 respondents (51.4%), biasing the calibration target toward politically engaged respondents who answer everything (flagged in build_log). Post-drop per-wave N is highly uneven: 2000 has N=263 (yet anchors the entire 2000 decade bucket with 2004+2008), and 1990 has N=540 with Form-B-only cultural items (~half the wave). The §11 bands are set as a flat point-estimate ±0.07 regardless of whether a decade rests on N=263 or N=5018, so a band built on the noisy 2000 wave is treated as equally authoritative as one built on 2020 (N=5018). The scorer reports CI95 on the engine's seed spread but the TARGET's sampling uncertainty is collapsed to a fixed ±0.07.
evidence: data/phase9_empirical/raw/anes_2d_ideology_methodology.md:156 (44,308->22,761, 51.4%), :188-189 (engagement bias + Form-split waves); per-wave post-listwise N (computed): 2000=263, 1990=540, 2020=5018; scripts/phase8f_lib.py:58 ('point estimate ±0.07'); scripts/anes_2d_compass.py:226-229 (listwise drop).
why_it_matters: A fit that 'passes' a band anchored on N=263 (2000) or a half-wave Form-B sample (1990) is far weaker than the uniform ±0.07 bands imply, and the engagement bias means the model is fit to opinionated respondents but presented as tracking 'US voters.' Both inflate apparent fidelity in the mid-period exactly where the common-mode channels were added to fix residuals.
suggested_fix: Scale each band's half-width by the wave's effective N (or bootstrap the target), surface the engaged-subpopulation caveat wherever the demo/methods say 'voters,' and avoid letting a single thin wave (2000) define a decade band.

stream_summary: Read against the actual canonical build (ANES_FULL_KWARGS = ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS in scripts/anes_preset.py:238), polarlab is structurally a one-way sorting/divergence machine. A full census of abm/rules/ shows exactly ONE endogenous convergence force in the shipped baseline — BoundedConfidenceInfluence — and on the sorted homophilous network it converges agents WITHIN camps (accelerating the sort), not across them. The one rule that could push people apart in a genuinely divergent (backfire) way, BacklashRepulsion, is constructed at strength=0.0 (historical_arc.py:1775) and is only switched on by the X1 intervention. Every other live rule is divergent/sorting (PartyPull, ActivistEliteCue, ConstraintOp, MediaConsumption, MediatedAnimus, the identity-alignment trio) or operates off the polarization axis (TieRewiring, ResidentialMigration, CohortReplacement, the two common-mode channels — which are explicitly "rigid sorting-INVARIANT" level shifts). The baseline cannot endogenously depolarize: out-party affect is monotonically cooling (the warming path in AffectiveUpdate needs cooperative edges that the baseline never seeds), and every dated event pushes toward divergence (Fox/Gingrich/social-media/Trump/status-threat) while the one convergence event, the Obama warmth bump, was retired (historical_arc.py:2009-2014) leaving only a tiny transient S-911 affect blip (shocks.py:476). Depolarization happens ONLY through the X1–X7 levers, and the two that "work" are externally-imposed resets (X5 one-shot HALVES party centroids+cue; X6 ADDS cross-party ties and RESETS affect/threat to 0) — not emergent recovery. This is a substantive, falsifiable claim about US politics (polarization is a ratchet) that is implicit in the architecture rather than stated as a headline model assumption. Separately, the headline "emergence" claim is overstated: the live honesty budget shows party_sep is only 0.28 free-flowing / 0.72 forcing-driven, contradicting the blindspot-#7 doc's "emergent 1.00 / ~0% input-carried."
title: The shipped model is a structural one-way ratchet: only one endogenous convergence force, and it converges within camps not across them
claim: Across the whole canonical pipeline there is exactly one rule that can endogenously bring agents together — BoundedConfidenceInfluence — and because influence flows only along network ties (ADR-001) and the network is homophilously sorted by TieRewiring, its neighbour-averaging pulls agents toward their OWN-party local mean, i.e. it is a within-camp convergence force that accelerates sorting rather than a cross-camp bridge. There is no rule in the baseline whose net effect is to reduce party separation or warm out-party affect. The model can therefore only move in the polarizing direction under its own dynamics.
evidence: Census of abm/rules/ on ANES_FULL_KWARGS (scripts/anes_preset.py:238 -> ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS). DIVERGENT/sorting & live: PartyPull (party_pull.py, strength 0.2532), ActivistEliteCue (activist_elite.py, gain 1.77, pushes elites apart along a frozen axis), ConstraintOp (constraint_op.py, 'self-reinforcement is the point... as vectors align, u_a sharpens, which aligns vectors further'), MediaConsumption (media_consumption.py), MediatedAnimus (mediated_animus.py, monotone cooling), IdentityToIdeologyPull / MeasuredAlignment / IdentitySorting / FactionAnchor. CONVERGENT & live: only BoundedConfidenceInfluence (influence.py:64 neighbours = neighbor_agents, network-mediated) + the FJ anchor term in GaussianNoise (noise.py:131, pulls to fixed 1980 anchor = freezes, does not depolarize). The one backfire/divergence rule is OFF: BacklashRepulsion(strength=0.0) (historical_arc.py:1775).
why_it_matters: If the model can only polarize under its own dynamics, then it is not a model of 'what drives polarization vs depolarization' — it is a model of how fast an assumed-monotone sort proceeds. That is a strong, contestable empirical claim (US affect/issue polarization has had plateaus and the cross-national record shows depolarizing cases, per the model's own blindspot #5 citing Boxell-Gentzkow-Shapiro 2024). A peer reviewer needs this stated as a top-level modeling assumption with its justification, not left implicit in which rule strengths happen to be zero.
suggested_fix: State explicitly in methods.md/README that the baseline has no endogenous depolarizing mechanism and that BC convergence is within-camp; justify the monotone-ratchet assumption against the empirical depolarizing/plateau record (the model's own #5) or add an endogenous convergence channel (e.g. cross-cutting-tie warming active in baseline) and show it doesn't reproduce the arc — either way make the assumption a falsifiable headline, not an emergent side effect of strength=0 knobs.

verification.reasoning:
I independently reproduced the canonical build (build_engine, ANES_FULL_KWARGS = ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS, seed 0) and ran the full 0..135 arc, measuring per-tick party separation (sep) and out-party warmth (aff_out). Results decisively support the finding:

(1) ENDOGENOUS AFFECT IS STRICTLY ONE-WAY. With exogenous_shocks=False (i.e. the model's OWN dynamics, no event handlers), out-party warmth has ZERO warming ticks across all 135 steps (largest per-step delta -0.0020 = pure monotone cooling). With shocks ON, the ONLY warming tick is step 64->65 (year ~2001.7, delta +0.0737) — confirmed to be the hardcoded S-911 exogenous shock, not a rule interaction. So the finding's central claim ("no rule whose net effect warms out-party affect; the only depolarizing move is exogenous") is literally true.

(2) SEP IS EFFECTIVELY MONOTONE. sep climbs 0.312 -> 1.065 (max 1.072@132). The 43/135 "decreasing" ticks are noise-level jitter (largest drop -0.0159 against a +0.75 total rise); sep never meaningfully reverses.

(3) THE ONE CITED CONVERGENCE FORCE IS INERT. Zeroing BoundedConfidenceInfluence (tier_c_bc_strength=0) changes end sep by only -0.0050 and end affect by only +0.0026 — and removing it slightly RAISES sep (1.0654->1.0704), so BC is not even reliably depolarizing (matches the code's own T0.6 "dead channel" note, anes_preset.py:75-77).

(4) THE DIVERGENT RULES ARE THE DRIVERS. PartyPull off -> end sep 1.065->0.421 (-0.645); ActivistEliteCue gain 0 -> 0.353 (-0.712). Confirms sep is produced by the sorting/divergent channels the finding lists.

(5) THE COMMON-MODE RULES DO NOT RESCUE IT. The finding's census stopped at ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS but didn't fully address CommonModeCulture/CommonModeEconomic (cultural_common_mode.py). I verified these are RIGID, SORTING-INVARIANT translations (rigid_common_mode_shift, lines 94-159): turning both off changes end sep by only +0.0113 (max 0.048 over the arc). They move axis LEVEL, not party separation/affect — so they are not cross-camp convergence forces and do not refute the finding.

Cited numbers all check out: tier_c_party_pull_strength=0.2532 (anes_preset.py:177), elite_gain=1.7689 (:172), BacklashRepulsion(strength=0.0) (historical_arc.py:1775), BC network-mediated via neighbor_agents (influence.py:64), TieRewiring is homophilous (tie_rewiring.py:1-7), so BC averaging is within-camp.

ONE EVIDENCE CORRECTION (does not change the verdict): the finding's rule census OMITS PerceptionUpdate (correction_rate=0.01, historical_arc.py:1785), which IS a genuine animus-reducing channel — it corrects biased out-party perceptions toward observed positions, shrinking the perceived distance that feeds AffectiveUpdate's cooling. But my shocks-off run proves it is far too weak to ever produce a net warming tick, so the finding's CONCLUSION stands. AffectiveUpdate also has a positive-going cooperative path (affective_update.py:316-317), but cooperative_share/cooperative edges are never seeded in the baseline (only under interventions) — confirmed by grep (only comment mentions at historical_arc.py:875,1023). So the baseline has no live warming path.

DISCLOSURE / SEVERITY: The model_blindspots.md register touches adjacent corners (#1 media over-weight, #2 perception corrections don't durably warm affect, #5 cross-national depolarizing cases per Boxell-Gentzkow-Shapiro 2024) but NOWHERE states the architectural fact that there is no endogenous depolarizing channel, so the model can only polarize under its own dynamics. The finding's "why it matters" — that this is a top-level modeling assumption left implicit in which rule strengths are zero — is accurate. Severity major is correct: it is a structural/interpretive limitation that bounds what the model can claim ("how fast an assumed sort proceeds," not "what drives polarization vs depolarization"), but it is a known/defensible design choice for a US-1980-2025 calibration target (which did monotonically polarize), not a correctness bug, so not critical.
verification.notes: Scratch probes left under validation/audit/_advreview_ratchet_probe.py and _advreview_drivers_probe.py (per ROE, the only writable area). No abm/tests/scripts/docs files were modified. Suggest the author add this one-directionality as an explicit top-level entry in docs/model_blindspots.md and docs/methods.md (provenance E), since it currently has to be inferred from zeroed rule strengths (BacklashRepulsion strength=0.0) and the absence of any endogenous warming channel.

title: Out-party affect can only cool in the baseline: the warming path is dead code unless an intervention is applied
claim: AffectiveUpdate has a positive-going (warming) channel, but it fires only on a cooperative network edge AND when warmth is above threshold. The canonical historical-arc build never seeds any cooperative edges, so the warming branch never executes in the baseline — out-party affect is structurally monotone-cooling. Warming becomes reachable only when the X6 intervention adds cooperative cross-party ties. Combined with MediatedAnimus (no saturation, deliberately keeps biting after contact starves) this hard-wires affective polarization as a one-way collapse.
evidence: AffectiveUpdate warming requires network.is_cooperative(agent.id, neighbor.id) (affective_update.py:312-317). Grep for cooperative-edge creation in historical_arc.py returns only kwarg-doc and COOPERATIVE_MUTE wiring (lines 780, 875, 1023, 1714) — no add of cooperative edges in the baseline build. X6 mechanism 'Add 1 cross-party involuntary cooperative tie per agent ... reset out-party affect to 0' (INTERVENTIONS_OVERVIEW.md:260-268). MediatedAnimus docstring: 'this channel deliberately does not damp ... its whole purpose is to keep biting once the network has sorted' (mediated_animus.py:32-35).
why_it_matters: A model presented as capturing affective polarization dynamics actually encodes affect as a ratchet by construction: the only path to warmth is to inject it externally. Any claim that the model 'explains' the affect trajectory is weakened — it cannot explain a recovery it is structurally incapable of producing, and the honesty budget's 'affect 94% emergent' is emergence of monotone cooling, not of a two-sided dynamic.
suggested_fix: Either seed a baseline level of cooperative/cross-cutting contact so the warming channel is live (and re-bless), or document prominently that out-party affect is monotone-cooling by construction and that the 'emergent affect' claim refers only to the cooling rate/shape.

verification.reasoning: Mechanistic core fully verified. (1) AffectiveUpdate's warming branch fires only when network.is_cooperative(agent.id, neighbor.id) AND own_warmth >= coop_positive_threshold (affective_update.py:312-317). (2) The ONLY code that ever tags cooperative=True edges is _x6_setup, the X6 intervention (interventions_phase6.py:732; docstring 694-698). The canonical arc network is built by Network.homophilous(...) + generate_involuntary_edges(...) (historical_arc.py:1419-1428); the latter adds edges with involuntary=True only (network.py:210), Network.homophilous constructs with no cooperative arg (network.py:113-114, ctor 92-103 -> empty _cooperative set), and TieRewiring adds untagged edges (tie_rewiring.py:118). So no cooperative edges exist in the baseline -> the valence=+coop_positive_magnitude line (317) is dead code in the baseline. (3) MediatedAnimus is strictly cooling (mag = -lr*weight*alignment, mediated_animus.py:87) with no saturation by design (docstring 32-35), and is ACTIVE in canonical: ANES_FULL_KWARGS has evidence_regrade=True (anes_preset.py:131 via the COMMONMODE_ECON->...->FED chain) so its lr=0.014 (historical_arc.py:1735). (4) Empirical confirmation: the shipped baseline aff series (web_demo/cc-data.js runs.baseline.macro[*].aff, 136 ticks) has EXACTLY ONE tick-to-tick warming step in the whole run; aff goes -0.093 (tick0) -> -0.806 (tick135), monotone cooling everywhere except that one step. title: Party polarization is forced to be symmetric and single-direction by the loop's frozen axis, despite the literature (and the model's own #6/#10) finding asymmetry and axis-specific timing
claim: ActivistEliteCue fixes each party's amplification AXIS at the first tick to +/- the frozen 1980 align_u direction and reuses it forever, so both parties polarize along the same fixed econ:cultural ratio in opposite directions. This bakes in (a) effectively symmetric positional divergence and (b) cultural timing welded to economic timing. The registered blindspots #6 (asymmetric elite dynamics) and #10 (cultural mis-pacing) both flag this, and #10's own falsify-first test shows the single fixed-direction loop CANNOT reproduce the 2012-2020 cultural surge — yet the canonical build still ships the fixed-axis loop.
evidence: activist_elite.py:92-131: 'Each party amplification AXIS is fixed at the first tick ... and reused thereafter'; axes[pid] = base for R, -base for D (line 127) -> symmetric opposite directions on one axis. model_blindspots.md #6 'Modeling symmetric drift rules ignores the asymmetric feedback' (lines 40-44). model_blindspots.md #10:85 'the activist->elite->mass loop amplifies along a fixed 2D direction, so cultural timing is structurally bound to economic timing ... cue-route hybrid is FALSIFIED on H1.' Asymmetry that does exist (mob_asym 0.188) is a small mobilization tilt, not a directional/feedback asymmetry.
why_it_matters: The literature's signature empirical finding about US polarization is that it is ASYMMETRIC (Republicans moved further; Leonard 2021, Hacker-Pierson) and that the cultural axis accelerated late and separately. A loop that is structurally single-axis and near-symmetric cannot represent either, so the model's positional story is qualitatively the wrong shape on two of the most-cited features. This is more than a calibration gap — it is a structural constraint of the emergence mechanism that is the project's centerpiece.
suggested_fix: Treat #6 and #10 as understated structural limits of the shipped emergence loop (not just 'medium/low priority calibration'), and either implement the parked axis-decoupling / asymmetric-feedback loop or foreground in the headline that the emergent positional engine is symmetric and single-axis by construction.

title: Real-world drivers a polarization model should arguably have are absent, and two registered blindspots understate this; geography and economics enter only as non-polarizing side channels
claim: Several mechanisms a US-polarization model would be expected to carry are absent or defanged: (1) genuine geographic/residential sorting affects only social_coord (a tie-formation latent), never ideology or the partisan composition of places, so the 'Big Sort' cannot feed back into positions; (2) material/economic conditions (recessions, inequality) are not drivers at all — the econ axis moves only via an exogenous thermostatic mood curve that is explicitly 'rigid, sorting-INVARIANT' and does not touch party_sep/affect/identity; (3) candidate/campaign agency and the extremist electoral penalty (#3) are not modeled; (4) demographic realism beyond cohort turnover is thin. Blindspot #5 (inequality) is registered but the conclusion drawn ('don't model it') leaves the model with NO material-conditions channel at all, which is itself a limitation worth stating.
evidence: ResidentialMigration mutates only social_coord, not ideology/party (residential_migration.py:80-94). CommonModeEconomic/Culture are 'rigid, sorting-invariant translation' that 'do not enter party_sep / affect / identity_alignment' (cultural_common_mode.py:76-98; honesty_budget.json:2). Econ axis driven by exogenous policy-mood curve, amplitude 0.09, fed not emergent (cultural_common_mode.py:205-225; blindspot #9). No candidate/campaign rule in abm/rules/ (Glob list). Mass-elite extremist-penalty/leapfrogging registered as #3 but not implemented. Inequality registered #5 with 'treating inequality as a direct driver oversimplifies' -> not modeled.
why_it_matters: The model is presented (CLAUDE.md) as an ABM of US polarization across 'several coupled channels.' In practice the coupled channels are ideology/identity/affect/media/network — i.e. the SORTING channels — while the classic structural drivers (geography feeding positions, economic conditions, candidate strategy, the extremist penalty) are either absent or routed into sorting-invariant level shifts that by construction cannot affect the polarization metrics. title: Economic-axis 'validation' is circular: the partisan econ center is hard-snapped to the fitted target curve
claim: CommonModeEconomic (relax=1.0) snaps the partisan economic center-of-mass to economic_mood_offset(year) every tick, so the validation quantity the from-scratch ANES+GSS battery flagged as failing (blindspot #9, 'econ center pinned ~0') is now mechanically equal to its own fed target rather than an emergent result. The fix passes the battery by construction.
evidence: abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:246-254 (CommonModeEconomic.apply computes delta = relax*(target-current), relax=1.0 default L237). My run of the canonical ANES_FULL_KWARGS (seed 0, full 135 ticks): measured partisan econ-center vs economic_mood_offset(year) -> 1996: 0.0882 vs 0.0900 (diff 0.0018); 2012: 0.0408 vs 0.0405 (diff 0.0003); 2024: -0.0442 vs -0.0495 (diff 0.0053). The single free scalar ECON_MOOD_AMPLITUDE=0.09 is explicitly described (L211-215) as fit to 'land the mid-90s partisan econ center at ~+0.09'. Canonical build has economic_common_mode=True (scripts/anes_preset.py:224-238).
why_it_matters: A model output that is hard-snapped to a fitted curve cannot validate the model against that curve — the metric IS the input. The docstring is honest that this is a fed forcing (provenance N+E, L196-201), but a reader of the realism battery / honesty budget would see the econ axis 'fixed' without realizing the agreement is tautological and non-falsifiable. This is the textbook 'manufacturing fit' pattern the audit was asked to hunt for.
suggested_fix: Either (a) drive the econ center via a damped mechanism (relax<<1, FJ-respecting per-agent pull) so agreement is partial/emergent and the residual is informative, or (b) explicitly exclude the snapped quantity from the validation scorecard and label it 'fed, not validated' wherever the econ-center match is reported. At minimum, report the residual variance the snap removes so the circularity is visible.

title: Common-mode channels bypass FJ/stubbornness entirely, contradicting the stated damping discipline
claim: rigid_common_mode_shift writes a.state.attrs['issues'] and a.state.ideology directly with the FULL per-tick delta for every agent, with no (1-stubbornness) factor and no routing through the delta pipeline. A maximally-stubborn agent that the FJ machinery says should barely move is still translated by the full common-mode displacement every tick.
evidence: abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:110-127 (direct writes to issues/anchor_issues/ideology and ideology[axis]+=delta; no stubbornness anywhere in the function). Both channels are EnvRules so this is permitted by the EnvRule protocol carve-out (abm/core/rules.py:37-50), but it is inconsistent with 'FJ (1-stubbornness) damping at apply-site of every ideology-moving rule.' Total forced displacement is non-trivial: I measured the econ target's monotone path length over the run = 0.2025 compass units and cultural baseline shifting from +0.10 (born ~1940) toward -0.24 (born ~1995).
why_it_matters: FJ anchoring is the model's central claim for why 'most agents barely move; a thin tail moves freely.' Two live channels in the SHIPPED build move all agents uniformly regardless of stubbornness, partially defeating that mechanism on the very axes (econ level, cultural level) where the channels operate. It also means within-axis level dynamics are not subject to the calibrated anchoring the rest of the engine enforces.
suggested_fix: Apply the common-mode shift through the delta pipeline (or at minimum weight it by (1-stubbornness) and route the residual back), or document explicitly in methods.md that the common-mode level is a rigid frame shift exempt from FJ by design and quantify how much un-anchored displacement it injects.

title: I3 ('no direct outcome writes') is asserted but the test only lints arc event handlers, not the rules/shocks that actually write outcomes
claim: CLAUDE.md states 'I3 (no direct outcome writes outside the delta pipeline) is enforced by test.' The enforcing test only AST-walks functions named _event_*/_decade_* inside historical_arc.py and never inspects the rule classes or shock applicators that DO write ideology/issues/affect/identity_alignment directly. So the channels that bypass the pipeline are invisible to the guard.
evidence: tests/test_i3_no_outcome_writes.py:53-54 restricts scanning to 'node.name.startswith("_event_") or node.name.startswith("_decade_")'. Direct outcome writes that the lint never sees: abm/pillars/shocks.py:276 (affect[...]=), :293 (identity_alignment/threat=), :340-343 (issues/ideology=) — the code even self-flags 'an I3-flagged outcome write either way' at L332-333; and abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:110-123 (issues/ideology=). Canonical build turns on exogenous_shocks=True AND both common modes (scripts/anes_preset.py:139,197,226).
why_it_matters: The invariant is presented to readers as machine-enforced, which lends false confidence. In practice the only outcome writes that bypass FJ/the pipeline are precisely the ones the test cannot reach. The test docstring exempts 'typed channels by construction' — but that turns I3 from an enforced invariant into an unverified naming convention.
suggested_fix: Either broaden the lint to scan abm/rules/*.py and abm/pillars/shocks.py and explicitly allowlist the sanctioned EnvRule/shock writes (so additions are caught), or soften the CLAUDE.md/methods wording to 'I3 is enforced for arc event handlers; typed input channels are exempt by convention, not test.'

verification.reasoning:
Every concrete claim in the finding checks out against the code.

1) The lint is scoped to arc handlers only. tests/test_i3_no_outcome_writes.py:48 reads ONLY `Path(arc.__file__).read_text()` — it never parses abm/pillars/shocks.py or abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py. Line 51-54 then walks only top-level `ast.FunctionDef` nodes whose name starts with `_event_`/`_decade_`. So the source files containing the actual outcome writes are never even loaded by the test.

2) The bypassing writes exist and target I3 outcome vars (OUTCOME_VARS = {affect, issues, ideology, identity_alignment, aff_out}, test line 28):
   - abm/pillars/shocks.py:276 `affect[other_party] = after`; :293 `a.state.attrs[attr] = after` (attr = perceived_threat / identity_alignment); :340-341 `a.state.attrs["issues"] = v_new` + `a.state.ideology = project1(...)`; :343 `a.state.ideology = engine.space.clip(new)`. These live inside the closure `event_fn` (shocks.py:362-451), NOT a `_event_*`/`_decade_*` function, so the lint's name filter excludes them even if it parsed the file. The code self-documents this at shocks.py:332-333: "(an I3-flagged outcome write either way — S3 re-expresses shocks as typed inputs)" — the author concedes these ARE I3 writes the test doesn't see.
   - abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:110-116 writes `a.state.attrs["issues"]`, `a.state.attrs["anchor_issues"]`, `a.state.ideology` directly inside `rigid_common_mode_shift`, called from `CommonModeCulture.apply` / `CommonModeEconomic.apply`.

3) These bypass the delta pipeline AND FJ damping, not just the lint. engine.py:46-51: env_rules run in the "Env phase" via `env_rule.apply(...)` BEFORE the agent/delta phase (engine.py:53-56). They mutate state directly, never producing a StateDelta, so the FJ `(1-stubbornness)` apply-site damping (applied only in the delta apply loop) does not touch them. CLAUDE.md's I3 wording is literally "no direct outcome writes outside the delta pipeline" — these are exactly that.

4) The canonical shipped build turns all three on. scripts/anes_preset.py: ANES_FULL_KWARGS = ANES_FULL_COMMONMODE_ECON_KWARGS (line 238), which chains exogenous_shocks=True (line 139), cultural_common_mode=True (line 197), economic_common_mode=True (line 226). historical_arc.py:1928,1935-1948 appends ShockRelaxation + CommonModeCulture + CommonModeEconomic to env_rules when those flags are set. So on the build the web demo serves, the only outcome writes that bypass the FJ/delta pipeline are precisely the ones the test cannot reach.

5) It is the sole I3 enforcement. Grep of tests/ for I3 finds only test_i3_no_outcome_writes.py (test_phase8c_affect.py is unrelated). And the lint is currently near-vacuous as a guard: of the 20 `_event_*`/`_decade_*` handlers in historical_arc.py, none write an OUTCOME_VAR (the one historical affect write, Obama-2008, was deleted per the docstring), so the test would pass on an empty violation list regardless — it guards a class that is already empty by construction while the live bypass channels sit outside its scope.

Skeptical counter-check: the test docstring argues the shock channel + data-fed series are "typed input channels ... exempt by construction." That is a defensible *design* position, but it does not rescue the CLAUDE.md/methods claim that I3 is "enforced by test." The exemption is asserted in prose, not verified; the rigid common-mode writes are not a "typed declarative shock" channel at all (they are a rule writing state.ideology directly), yet are equally invisible to the guard. So the finding's why_it_matters — "an enforced invariant turned into an unverified naming convention" — is accurate.

Severity: I leave it at major (not critical). It is a documentation/assurance defect, not a numerical bug: the bypassing writes are intentional, gated, and documented elsewhere as exogenous forcings, and they are sorting-invariant by design (rigid translation). The harm is overclaim — readers are told a structural invariant is machine-checked when the channels that violate its letter are exactly the unchecked ones. That undermines the honesty discipline the project trades on, but does not by itself corrupt a shipped number.

title: Common-mode shift is not exactly sorting-invariant and silently drags Independents who are excluded from the target
claim: The 'rigid translation is sorting-invariant' claim has two leaks: (1) the np.clip(v+bump,-1,1) on issue items breaks rigidity for agents near the boundary, so sep/corr/wp_sd shift slightly per snap; (2) target and current are computed over PARTISANS only (_partisan_mean_axis / partisan birth-mean) but the shift is applied to ALL agents including Independents, so Independents are puppeted by a partisan-defined thermostat.
evidence: Isolated test of a manual +0.10 econ shift at mid-period (wp_sd~0.41): sep delta -1.4e-3, corr delta +1.6e-3, wp_sd delta -2.0e-3, and the partisan center moved by 0.0988 not 0.10 (clip leakage). Independent-cloud effect over full run: 2025 Independent econ-center = -0.0326 with econ channel on vs -0.0021 with it off (diff -0.031), driven entirely by the partisan-targeted shift. Code: abm/rules/cultural_common_mode.py:87-91 (partisan-only mean), :107-127 (applied to all agents), :110 (clip). End-state issue saturation is 0% so the clip leak is small at the endpoint but larger mid-period.
why_it_matters: The invariance argument in the docstring (and the methods provenance) is the justification for adding the channel without disturbing the calibrated differential. The leaks are small per-tick but accumulate over 135 snaps and are largest exactly mid-period where the diagnosed level error was claimed fixed. The Independent drag is an undocumented side effect: Independents have zero-mean identities and centrist diets but are still moved -0.031 econ by a channel whose target excludes them.
suggested_fix: State the invariance is approximate (clip-limited) and quantify residual sep/corr drift over the run; and either include Independents in the target computation or exclude them from the shift, and document the choice.

title: Method-B published baseline shows an ensemble-mean macro over a cross-seed-subsample cloud; the macro lines are not computable from the displayed dots for nonlinear metrics
claim: The shipped baseline displays an agent cloud that is a uniform 250-agent subsample pooled across 8 different seeds, while the macro scalar lines (party_sep, within_party_sd, variance, constraint, modularity, corr) are the ensemble MEAN of per-seed values. title: Raw out-party affect overflows the [-1,1] warmth floor (-1.27) and is hidden by a clip-on-read
claim: Out-party warmth is stored UNBOUNDED in agent state and only clipped to [-1,1] when a metric/consumer reads it (abm/rules/affective_update.py:289 'cumulative affect is clipped by the metric reader at [-1,1]'; abm/metrics/affective.py:47 np.clip per value). The unclipped partisan out-party mean reaches -1.266 by tick 135 (2025) and -1.115 by 2022. Because the metric averages per-value-clipped warmth over ALL agents incl. Independents and isolated zero-warmth agents, the published/graded value (-0.807 at 2020) is materially less cold than the underlying state (-1.115), so the clip masks the true magnitude of the over-animus.
evidence: validation/run_canonical.json macro.affect range = -0.093 (1980) to -1.266 (2025), computed in validation/gen_run.py as the unclipped partisan out-party mean. affective_update.py:289 comment; affective.py:41-50 averages clipped per-value warmth over all agents.
why_it_matters: A warmth scale defined on [-1,1] (ANES neutral-centered) that internally runs to -1.27 means the dynamics have no real attractor/saturation — the negative-going AffectiveUpdate just keeps accumulating. The clip-on-read converts an unphysical state into a plausible-looking output, so the metric understates how far off-scale the affect dynamics are. This is exactly the kind of silent saturation a peer reviewer should flag.
suggested_fix: Bound affect in-state (saturating update toward a -1 asymptote, e.g. tanh or a per-tick cap) rather than clipping only at read time, so the published number reflects the true dynamics; document the saturation form in methods.md.

title: Model over-produces CULTURAL-axis separation relative to ECONOMIC: cult/econ ratio ~1.0 vs ANES ~0.66
claim: The model allocates separation roughly equally across axes (mean cultural/economic centroid-gap ratio = 0.999 over 1986-2024), while ANES has cultural sorting consistently SMALLER than economic (mean ratio 0.655). Economic-axis fit is good (mean |R-D gap error| = 0.056) but cultural-axis gap is over-produced (mean |error| = 0.132, ~2.4x worse), e.g. 1990 model cult gap 0.315 vs ANES 0.127 (+0.187), 2012 0.676 vs 0.482 (+0.194), 2016 0.764 vs 0.538 (+0.225). Per-axis gaps are NOT a calibration target (s4_targets.py fits combined party_sep + the 2D Wasserstein cloud + ANES bands), so this is an independent degree of freedom.
why_it_matters: The combined party_sep fit hides a structural mis-allocation: the model gets the right total separation by inflating the cultural axis and slightly deflating the economic axis. Empirically the US sorted more on economics than on culture over most of this window; the model inverts/flattens that. Any axis-resolved interpretation in the demo (the compass x=econ/y=cult is the model's central visual) is therefore distorted even though the aggregate looks calibrated.
suggested_fix: Add a per-axis fit gate (econ-gap and cult-gap separately) to the realism battery rather than only combined sep + 2D cloud; investigate the cultural-channel forcing (cultural_common_mode + the single-axis loop) that is pushing cult sorting up.

So neither channel touches the per-axis party GAP, and no other mechanism differentiates the cultural-axis gap from the economic-axis gap -- the sorting force (activist->elite->mass loop + PartyPull) acts symmetrically on the 7D substrate, projected to 2D. verification.reasoning:
I verified the model's numbers exactly and then tested the finding's central claim against the project's own raw ANES individual-level data.

MODEL NUMBERS CONFIRMED. From validation/run_canonical.json (verified faithful: its last tick reproduces econ_com=-0.049964, cult_com=-0.047441, sep=1.065354, and I re-ran validation/_fingerprint.py canonical against the live shipped engine -> hash 6c5bdb215fb9... matching the cited canonical fingerprint). Partisan econ-cult corr: 1986 +0.475, 2020 +0.780, 2024 +0.818 -- exactly the finding's figures.

CENTRAL CLAIM REFUTED. The finding asserts corr ~0.8 is unrealistic and "incompatible" with ANES, citing the project's docs ("realism B2: corr(x,y) ~0.78 vs ANES ~0.5-0.6", docs/results/e5_holdout.md:72; "corr 0.78 ... vs ~0.21 Treier-Hillygus", docs/results/realism_report.md:99). I computed the REAL ANES partisan-pooled econ-cult correlation directly from the project's own individual-level file data/phase9_empirical/derived/respondent_coordinates.csv (header year,party_7pt,party,weight,econ,cult). Real partisan-pooled correlation RISES: 1986 +0.359 -> 2012 +0.638 -> 2020 +0.776 -> 2024 +0.778. Side-by-side model vs real over 14 ANES waves (1986-2024): MAE = 0.048, mean signed error = +0.005 (essentially unbiased). At 2004 the model UNDER-correlates (model +0.526 vs real +0.586). Like-with-like at the endpoint: MODEL 2025 full-pop corr +0.798 vs REAL ANES 2024 all-respondents corr +0.768 (gap +0.03). So the model's corr ~0.8 is very close to what ANES actually shows; the docs' "ANES ~0.5-0.6" benchmark is contradicted by the repo's own raw respondent data (the ~0.5 is a mid-period value; the ~0.21 is a Treier-Hillygus 2009 slope, a different statistic from a paper whose data ends ~2004 -- not the 2020s endpoint correlation).

WHAT SURVIVES (partial, separate issue). The finding's secondary evidence ("econ leads cult") is real but is a DIFFERENT quantity from the correlation: the cult/econ centroid-GAP RATIO. I computed model vs real: model ratio runs ~0.6 (1986) -> ~1.0-1.3 (mid-period) -> ~0.97 (2024), while real runs ~0.38 -> ~0.7-0.9 -> ~0.93. So the model does over-produce cultural sorting relative to economic in the mid-period. But (a) this is the gap-magnitude allocation, not the correlation; (b) the finding's causal chain ("the corr is the upstream cause of the cultural mis-allocation") is asserted, not demonstrated -- a high within-population correlation does not force cult_gap == econ_gap (the gaps are between-party centroid differences, governed by the loop's per-axis amplification, not by the within-cloud corr). The finding bundles a refuted headline (corr unrealistic) with a partially-true secondary observation (mid-period cult/econ ratio bias) under a single mechanistic story that does not hold up.

Downgrading severity to minor: the empirically over-claimed part (axes are "nearly the same axis by 2024, unrealistically rigid") is false against the data; what remains is a documented, smaller mid-period gap-ratio bias the project already flags.

Headline: the ECONOMIC axis fits well (mean |centroid-gap error| 0.056 over 1986-2024), but the model systematically (a) over-produces OUT-PARTY AFFECT at every decade — out of the ANES thermometer band 1980/1990/2000/2010/2020, growing from 0.7x to 3.1x the band halfwidth, with the raw partisan value running to -1.27 (below the -1 floor, masked by a clip-on-read); (b) over-produces CULTURAL-axis separation relative to economic (mean |cult error| 0.132, ~2.4x the econ error), collapsing the real cult/econ ratio of ~0.66 to ~1.0; and (c) under-produces party-cloud overlap / bimodality structure. The per-axis decomposition is genuinely independent of the calibration (which targets combined party_sep + the 2D Wasserstein cloud + affect bands, not per-axis gaps), so these are real degrees of freedom the model mis-allocates. Two of these contradict claims in the author's own realism_report.md.
title: Model under-produces party-cloud overlap and only becomes bimodal in the very late period; ANES shows significant dips throughout
claim: The model's combined-partisan distribution crosses the bimodality-coefficient threshold (BC>0.555) only at 2020+ on the economic axis (econ 0.560/0.560 at 2020/2024) and never clearly on cultural (peaks 0.553 at 2024); 1986-2008 are all unimodal (BC 0.43-0.49). ANES, by contrast, shows statistically significant Hartigan dips (p<0.001) on the economic axis from 1986 onward. The model reproduces the mean gap but not the early-period distributional separation. A 2D histogram-overlap proxy also reads far lower than ANES ovl_2d, though that specific number is sensitive to binning (1D KDE overlap is more moderate: econ 0.68->0.28, cult 0.76->0.22, 1986->2024).
evidence: validation/audit/fit_battery.py bimodality section; ANES dip_econ p-values <0.001 in data/phase9_empirical/derived/polarization_series.csv. Caveat: BC (model) vs Hartigan dip (ANES) are different statistics and model n=220 partisans vs ANES n=hundreds-thousands, so this is an observation not a hard miss.
why_it_matters: The real population was already detectably two-humped on economics in the late 1980s; the model only develops that structure 30 years later, suggesting its early-period dynamics are too unimodal/Gaussian (driven by GaussianNoise + FJ anchoring around a single seed) even when the centroid gap is right. Distributional shape matters for any claim about 'sorting into camps'.
suggested_fix: Add a per-axis dip/bimodality check to the realism battery (currently only within_party_sd and 2D Wasserstein are graded); compare on a matched statistic (compute Hartigan dip on the model's partisan distribution, not BC).
